Q 49

Solve: {tex}\frac{3 x-8}{2 x}{/tex} = 1

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{3 x-8}{2 x}{/tex} = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x - 8 = 2x [by cross - multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x - 2x = 8 [transposing 2x to LHS and 8 to RHS]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 8 

Q 50

Solve: {tex}\frac{5x}{2 x-1}{/tex} = 2

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{5x}{2 x-1}{/tex} = 2
5x = 2(2x - 1)[by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = 4x - 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x - 4x = -2 [transposing 4x to LHS]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -2

Q 51

Solve: {tex}\frac{2 x-3}{4 x+5}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex}

Solution

W have, {tex}\frac{2 x-3}{4 x+5}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3(2x - 3) = 4x + 5 [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x - 9 = 4x + 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x - 4x = 9 + 5 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 x}{2}=\frac{14}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 7

Q 52

Solve: {tex}\frac{8}{x}=\frac{5}{x-1}{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{8}{x}=\frac{5}{x-1}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8(x - 1) = 5x  [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8x - 8 = 5x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8x - 5x = 8 [transposing 5x to LHS and -8 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{3 x}{3}=\frac{8}{3}{/tex} [dividing both sides by 3]
{tex}\therefore \quad x=\frac{8}{3}{/tex}

Q 53

Solve: {tex}\frac{5(1-x)+3(1+x)}{1-2 x}=8{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{5(1-x)+3(1+x)}{1-2 x}=8{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5 (1 - x) + 3 (1 + x) = 8 (1 - 2x) [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5 - 5x + 3 + 3x = 8 - 16x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8 - 2x = 8 -16x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16x - 2x = 8 - 8 [transposing -16x to LHS and 8 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 14x = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{14 x}{14}=\frac{0}{14}{/tex} [dividing both sides by 14]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 0

Q 54

Solve: {tex}\frac{0.2 x+5}{3.5 x-3}=\frac{2}{5}{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{0.2 x+5}{3.5 x-3}=\frac{2}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5(0.2x + 5) = 2(3.5x - 3) [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 25 = 7x - 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x - 7x = - 6 - 25 [transposing lx to LHS and 25 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -6x = -31
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{-6 x}{-6}=\frac{-31}{-6}{/tex} [dividing both sides by -6 ]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{31}{6}{/tex}

Q 55

Solve: {tex}\frac{y-(4-3 y)}{2 y-(3+4 y)}=\frac{1}{5}{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{y-(4-3 y)}{2 y-(3+4 y)}=\frac{1}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5(y - 4 + 3y) = 2y - 3 - 4y [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  5 (4y - 4) = - 3 - 2y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 20y - 20 = - 3 - 2y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 20y + 2y = 20 - 3 [transposing -20 to RHS and -2y to LHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 22y = 17
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{22 y}{22}=\frac{17}{22}{/tex} [dividing both sides by 22]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{17}{22}{/tex}

Q 56

Solve: {tex}\frac{x}{5}=\frac{x-1}{6}{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{x}{5}=\frac{x-1}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  6x = 5(x - 1) [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x = 5x - 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x - 5x = -5 [transposing 5x to LHS]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -5

Q 57

Solve: 0.4 (3x - 1) = 0.5x + 1

Solution

Given, 0.4 (3x - 1) = 0.5x + 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1.2x - 0.4 = 0.5x + 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1.2x - 0.5x = 1+ 0.4 [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0.7x = 1.4 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{0.7 x}{0.7}=\frac{1.4}{0.7}{/tex}  [dividing both sides by 0.7]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2

Q 58

Solve: 8x - 7 - 3x = 6x - 2x - 3

Solution

Given, 8x - 7 - 3x = 6x - 2x - 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8x - 3x - 6x + 2x = - 3 + 7 [transposing 6x, -2x to LHS and -7 to RHS]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 4

Q 59

Solve: 10x - 5 - 7x = 5x + 15 - 8

Solution

Given, 10x - 5 - 7x = 5x + 15 - 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10x - 7x - 5x = 5 + 15 - 8 [transposing 5x to LHS and -5 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2x = 12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{-2 x}{-2}=\frac{12}{-2}{/tex} [dividing both sides by -2]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -6

Q 60

Solve: 4t - 3 - (3t + 1) = 5t - 4

Solution

Given, 4t - 3 - (3t + 1) = 5t - 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4t - 3 - 3t - 1 = 5t - 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t - 4 = 5t - 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t - 5t = - 4 + 4 [transposing 5t to LHS and -4 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 4t = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{-4 t}{-4}=\frac{0}{-4}{/tex} [dividing both sides by -4]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} t = 0

Q 61

Solve: 5(x - 1) - 2 (x + 8) = 0

Solution

Given, 5(x - 1) - 2 (x + 8) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x - 5 - 2x - 16 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x - 21 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 21 [transposing -21 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{3 x}{3}=\frac{21}{3}{/tex} [dividing both sides by 3]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 7

Q 62

Solve: {tex}\frac{x}{2}-\frac{1}{4}\left(x-\frac{1}{3}\right)=\frac{1}{6}(x+1)+\frac{1}{12}{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{x}{2}-\frac{1}{4}\left(x-\frac{1}{3}\right)=\frac{1}{6}(x+1)+\frac{1}{12}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{x}{2}-\frac{x}{4}+\frac{1}{12}=\frac{x}{6}+\frac{1}{6}+\frac{1}{12}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{2 x-x}{4}+\frac{1}{12}=\frac{x}{6}+\frac{2+1}{12}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{x}{4}+\frac{1}{12}=\frac{x}{6}+\frac{3}{12}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{x}{4}-\frac{x}{6}=\frac{3}{12}-\frac{1}{12}{/tex} [transposing {tex}\frac{x}{6}{/tex} to LHS and {tex}\frac{1}{12}{/tex}to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{6 x-4 x}{24}=\frac{3-1}{12}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{2 x}{24}=\frac{2}{12}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 \times 12 x=2 \times 24{/tex} [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 24x = 48
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{24 x}{24}=\frac{48}{24}{/tex} [dividing both sides by 24]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2

Q 63

Solve: {tex}\frac{1}{2}(x+1)+\frac{1}{3}(x-1)=\frac{5}{12}(x-2){/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{1}{2}(x+1)+\frac{1}{3}(x-1)=\frac{5}{12}(x-2){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{x}{2}+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{x}{3}-\frac{1}{3}=\frac{5 x}{12}-\frac{5}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{x}{2}+\frac{x}{3}-\frac{5 x}{12}=\frac{1}{3}-\frac{1}{2}-\frac{5}{6}{/tex} [ transposing {tex}\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{3}{/tex}to RHS and {tex}\frac{5x}{12}{/tex}to LHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{6 x+4 x-5 x}{12}=\frac{2-3-5}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{5 x}{12}=\frac{-6}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 5 x \times 6=(-6) \times 12{/tex} [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{(-6) \times 12}{5 \times 6}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad x=\frac{-12}{5}{/tex}

Q 64

Solve: {tex}\frac{x+1}{4}=\frac{x-2}{3}{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{x+1}{4}=\frac{x-2}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3(x + 1) = 4(x - 2) [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 3 = 4x - 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x - 4x = -8 - 3 [transposing 4x to LHS and 3 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -x = -11 [dividing both sides by -1]
{tex}\Rightarrow\frac{-x}{-1}=\frac{-11}{-1}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 11

Q 65

Solve: {tex}\frac{2 x-1}{5}=\frac{3 x+1}{3}{/tex}

Solution

We have, {tex}\frac{2 x-1}{5}=\frac{3 x+1}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3(2x - 1) = 5(3x + 1) [by cross-multiplication], we get
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x - 3 = 15x + 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x - 15x = 3 + 5 [transposing 15x to LHS and -3 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 9x = 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{-9 x}{-9}=\frac{8}{-9}{/tex} [dividing both sides by -9]
{tex}\therefore \quad x=\frac{-8}{9}{/tex}

Q 66

Solve: 1 - (x - 2) - [(x - 3) - (x - 1)] = 0

Solution

Given, 1 - (x - 2) - [(x - 3) - (x - 1)] = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 - x + 2 - [ x - 3 - x + 1] = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 - x -[-2] = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 - x + 2 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - x + 5 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -x = - 5 [transposing 5 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{-x}{-1}=\frac{-5}{-1}{/tex} [dividing both sides by -1]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 5

Q 67

Solve: {tex}3 x-\frac{x-2}{3}=4-\frac{x-1}{4}{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}3 x-\frac{x-2}{3}=4-\frac{x-1}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{9 x-(x-2)}{3}=\frac{16-(x-1)}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4(9x - x + 2) = 3(16 -x + 1) [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4(8x + 2) = 3(- x + 17)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 32x + 8 = - 3x + 51
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 32x + 3x = 51 - 8 [transposing -3x to LHS and 8 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 35x = 43
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{35 x}{35}=\frac{43}{35}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad x=\frac{43}{35}{/tex}

Q 68

Solve: {tex}\frac{3 t+5}{4}-1=\frac{4 t-3}{5}{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{3 t+5}{4}-1=\frac{4 t-3}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{3 t+5-4}{4}=\frac{4 t-3}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5(3t + 5 - 4)= 4(4t - 3) [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5(3t + 1) = 4 (4t-3 )
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 15t+ 5 = 16t -12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 15t -16t = - 12 - 5 [transposing 16t to LHS and 5 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - t = -17
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{-t}{-1}=\frac{-17}{-1}{/tex} [dividing both sides by -1]
{tex}\therefore \quad t=17{/tex}

Q 69

Solve: {tex}\frac{2 y-3}{4}-\frac{3 y-5}{2}=y+\frac{3}{4}{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{2 y-3}{4}-\frac{3 y-5}{2}=y+\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{2 y-3-2(3 y-5)}{4}=\frac{4 y+3}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2y - 3 - 6y + 10 = 4y + 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 4y + 7 = 4y + 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 4y - 4y = 3 - 7 [transposing 4y to LHS and 7 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 8y = - 4
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{-8 y}{-8}=\frac{-4}{-8}{/tex} [dividing both sides by -8]
{tex}\therefore \quad y=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

Q 70

Solve: 0.25 (4x - 5) = 0.75x + 8

Solution

Given, 0.25 (4x - 5) = 0.75x + 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x - 1.25 = 0.75x + 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x - 0.75x = 1.25 + 8 [transposing 0.75x to LHS and 1.25 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0.25x = 9.25
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{0.25 x}{0.25}=\frac{9.25}{0.25}{/tex} [dividing both sides by 0.25]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 37

Q 71

Solve: {tex}\frac{9-3 y}{1-9 y}=\frac{8}{5}{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{9-3 y}{1-9 y}=\frac{8}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5(9 - 3y) = 8(1 - 9y) [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 45 - 15y = 8 - 72y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 72y- 15y = 8 - 45 [transposing -72y to LHS and 45 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 57y = - 37
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{57 y}{57}=\frac{-37}{57}{/tex} [dividing both sides by 57]
{tex}\therefore \quad y=\frac{-37}{57}{/tex}

Q 72

Solve: {tex}\frac{3 x+2}{2 x-3}=-\frac{3}{4}{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{3 x+2}{2 x-3}=-\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  4 (3x + 2) = - 3 (2x - 3) [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12x + 8 = - 6x + 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12x + 6x = 9 - 8 [transposing -6x to LHS and 8 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}18x = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{18 x}{18}=\frac{1}{18}{/tex} [dividing both sides by 18]
{tex}\therefore \quad x=\frac{1}{18}{/tex}

Q 73

Solve: {tex}\frac{5 x+1}{2 x}=-\frac{1}{3}{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{5 x+1}{2 x}=-\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 (5x + 1) = - 2x [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 15x + 3 = - 2x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 15x + 2x = - 3 [transposing -2x to LHS and 3 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 17x = - 3
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{17 x}{17}=\frac{-3}{17}{/tex}  [dividing both sides by 17]
{tex}\therefore \quad x=\frac{-3}{17}{/tex}

Q 74

Solve: {tex}\frac{3 t-2}{3}+\frac{2 t+3}{2}=t+\frac{7}{6}{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{3 t-2}{3}+\frac{2 t+3}{2}=t+\frac{7}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{2(3 t-2)+3(2 t+3)}{6}=\frac{6 t+7}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6t - 4 + 6t + 9 = 6t + 7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12t + 5 = 6t + 7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  12t - 6t = 7 -5 [transposing 6t to LHS and 5 to RHS] 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6t = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{6 t}{6}=\frac{2}{6}{/tex} [dividing both sides by 6]
{tex}\therefore \quad t=\frac{1}{3}{/tex}

Q 75

Solve:
{tex}m - \frac{{m - 1}}{2} = 1 - \frac{{m - 2}}{3}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\frac{{2m - m + 1}}{2} = \frac{{3 - m + 2}}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{m + 1}}{2} = \frac{{5 - m}}{3}{/tex}
Using    cross - multiplication
3(m + 1) = 2(5 - m)
3m + 3 = 10 - 2m
3m + 2m = 10 - 3
5m = 7
m = 7/5 

Q 76

Solve: 4(3p + 2) - 5 (6p - 1) = 2 (p - 8) - 6 (7p - 4)

Solution

Given, 4 (3p + 2) - 5 (6p - 1) = 2 (p - 8) - 6 (7p - 4)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12p + 8 - 30p + 5 = 2p -16 - 42p + 24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -18 p+ 13 = - 40p + 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -18p + 40p = 8 -13 [transposing -40p to LHS and 13 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 22p = - 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{22 p}{22}=\frac{-5}{22}{/tex} [dividing both sides by 22]
{tex}\therefore \quad p=\frac{-5}{22}{/tex}

Q 77

Solve: 3(5x - 7) + 2(9x - 11) = 4(8x - 7) - 111

Solution

Given, 3(5x - 7) + 2(9x - 11) = 4(8x - 7) - 111
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 15x - 21 + 18x - 22 = 32x - 28 - 111
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 33x - 43 = 32x - 139
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 33x - 32x = - 139 + 43 [transposing 32x to LHS and -43 to RHS]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = - 96

Q 78

Solve: 0.16 (5x - 2 ) = 0.4x + 7

Solution

Given, 0.16 (5x - 2) = 0.4x + 7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0.8x - 0.32 = 0.4x + 7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0.8x - 0.4x = 0.32 + 7 [transposing 0.4x to LHS and - 0.32 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0.4x = 7.32
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{0.4 x}{0.4}=\frac{7.32}{0.4}{/tex} [dividing both sides by 0.4]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 18.3

Q 79

Radha takes some flowers in a basket and visits three temples one-by-one. At each temple, she offers one-half of the flowers from the basket. If she is left with 3 flowers at the end, then find the number of flowers she had in the beginning.

Solution

Let total number of the flowers be x.
Then flowers offered in first temple = {tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex}
Flowers offered in second temple = {tex}\frac{x}{4}{/tex}
Flowers offered in third temple = {tex}\frac{x}{8}{/tex}
Number of flowers left at the end = 3
According to the question, {tex}x-\left(\frac{x}{2}+\frac{x}{4}+\frac{x}{8}\right)=3{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{8 x-(4 x+2 x+x)}{8}=3{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8x - 7x = 24
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 24
Hence, total number of flowers she had in the beginning is 24.

Q 80

₹ 13500 are to be distributed among Salma, Kiran, and Jenifer in such a way that Salma gets ₹ 1000 more than Kiran, and Jenifer gets ₹ 500 more than Kiran. Find the money received by Jenifer.

Solution

Let the money received by Kiran be ₹ x
Then, the money received by Salma = ₹(x + 1000)
and the money received by Jenifer = ₹(x + 500)
According to the question, x + x + 1000 + x + 500 = 13500
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 1500 = 13500
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 13500 -1500
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 12000
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{12000}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 4000
Hence, the money received by Jenifer = ₹ 4000 + 500 = ₹ 4500

Q 81

The volume of water in a tank is twice of that in the other. If we draw out 25 litres from the first and add it to the other, the volumes of the water in each tank will be the same. Find the volumes of water in each tank.

Solution

Let the volume of water in one tank be x L
Then, the volume of the water in another tank = 2x L
According to the question,
The volume of the water in the first tank + 25L = Volume of the water in another tank - 25L 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 25 = 2x - 25
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x - x = 25 + 25
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 50
Hence, the volume of water in one tank = 50 L
and the volume of the water in another tank = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 50
= 100 L

Q 82

Anushka and Aarushi are friends. They have equal amount of money in their pockets. Anushka gave {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} of her money to Aarushi as her birthday gift. Then, Aarushi gave a party at a restaurant and cleared the bill by paying half of the total money with her. If the remaining money in Aarushi's pocket is ₹1600, then find the sum gifted by Anushka.

Solution

Let Anushka and Aarushi have an equal amount of money in their pocket i.e. Rs. {tex} x{/tex}
After giving {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}of the money of Anushka to Aarushi, Amount of Aarushi = Rs. {tex}\left(x+\frac{x}{3}\right){/tex}
According to the question, {tex}\left(x+\frac{x}{3}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \times\left(x+\frac{x}{3}\right)=1600{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad\left(x+\frac{x}{3}\right)\left(1-\frac{1}{2}\right)=1600{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad\left(x+\frac{x}{3}\right) \times \frac{1}{2}=1600{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{3 x+x}{3}=1600 \times 2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{4 x}{3}=3200{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad x=3200 \times \frac{3}{4}=2400{/tex}
So, the money gifted by Anushka = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}of 2400
= {tex}\frac{1}{3} \times 2400{/tex}
= ₹800

Q 83

Kaustubh had 60 flowers. He offered some flowers in temple and found that the ratio of the number of remaining flowers to that of flowers in the beginning is 3 : 5. Find the number of flowers offered by him in the temple.

Solution

Let the number of flowers offered by Kaustubh in the temple be x
Then, the remaining flowers = 60 - x
According to the question, {tex}\frac{\text { Number of remaining flowers }}{\text { Number of flowers in beginning }}=\frac{3}{5}{/tex} [given]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{60-x}{60}=\frac{3}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 300 - 5x = 180
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = 300 - 180
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = 120
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{120}{5}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 24
Therefore, the number of flowers offered by Kaustubh in the temple is 24.

Q 84

The sum of three consecutive even natural numbers is 48. Find the greatest of these numbers.

Solution

Let the three consecutive even natural numbers be x, (x + 2) and (x + 4). According to the question,
x + (x + 2) + (x + 4) = 48 [given]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 6 = 48
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 48 - 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 42 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{42}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 14
Hence, the three consecutive even natural numbers are 14, (14 + 2) and (14 + 4), i.e. 14,16 and 18.
Therefore, the greatest number is 18.

Q 85

The sum of three consecutive odd natural numbers is 69. Find the prime number out of these numbers.

Solution

Let the three consecutive odd natural numbers be x, {x + 2) and {x + 4). According to the question,
x + (x + 2) + {x + 4) = 69 [given]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 6 = 69
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 69 - 6 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 63
{tex}\therefore \quad x=63 \times \frac{1}{3}=21{/tex}
Thus, the three consecutive odd natural numbers are 21, (21 + 2) and (21 + 4), i.e. 21, 23 and 25. Out of these, only 23 is the prime number.

Q 86

The sum of three consecutive numbers is 156. Find the number which is a multiple of 13 out of these numbers.

Solution

Let three consecutive numbers be x, (x + 1) and (x + 2).
According to the question,
x + (x + 1) + (x + 2) = 156 [given]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 3 = 156 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = 156 - 3 = 153
{tex}\therefore \quad x=153 \times \frac{1}{3}=51{/tex}
Thus, we get the numbers 51, 51 + 1 and 51 + 2, i.e. 51,52 and 53. Out of these, only 52 is a multiple of 13.

Q 87

Find a number whose fifth part increased by 30 is equal to its fourth part decreased by 30.

Solution

Let the number be x.
According to the question, {tex}\frac{x}{5}+30=\frac{x}{4}-30{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{x}{5}-\frac{x}{4}=-30-30{/tex} [transposing {tex}\frac{x}{4}{/tex} to LHS and 30 to RHS]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{4 x-5 x}{20}=-60{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -x = - 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 [by cross-multiplication]
x = 1200
Hence, the required number is 1200.

Q 88

Divide 54 into two parts, such that one part is {tex}\frac{2}{7}{/tex} of the other.

Solution

Let the other part be x. Then, first part will be {tex}\frac{2x}{7}{/tex} .
According to the question, {tex}x+\frac{2 x}{7}=54{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{7 x+2 x}{7}=54{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9x = 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 54 [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{7 \times 54}{9}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 42
Hence, the first part ={tex}\frac{2 \times 42}{7}{/tex}= 12
and the other part = 42

Q 89

Sum of the digits of a two-digit number is 11. The given number is less than the number obtained by interchanging the digits by 9. Find the number.

Solution

Let the unit’s digit be x.
Then, the ten’s digit = 11 - x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Number = 10(11 - x) + x
= 110 - 10x + x 
= 110 - 9x
Now, number obtained by interchanging the digits = 10x + (11 - x)
= 9x + 11
According to the given question,
9x + 11 - (110 - 9x) = 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x+ 11 - 110 + 9x = 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 18x = 9 - 11 + 110
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}18x = 108
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{108}{18}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 6
Hence, unit’s digit = 6
and ten’s digit = 11- 6
= 5
Hence, the required number is 56.

Q 90

Two equal sides of a triangle are each 4 m less than three times the third side. Find the dimensions of the triangle, if its perimeter is 55 m.

Solution

Let the third side of the triangle be x m.
Then, two equal sides of triangle = (3x - 4)m
Given, perimeter of triangle = 55m
{tex}\because{/tex} Perimeter of a triangle = Sum of the sides of the triangle
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x + 3x - 4 + 3x - 4 = 55
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7x - 8 = 55 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7x = 55 + 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7x = 63 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{63}{7}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 9
Hence, the dimensions of the triangle are 9 m, (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 - 4)m and (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 - 4)m, i.e. 9, (27 - 4), and (27 - 4)m, i.e. 9 m, 23 m and 23m.

Q 91

After 12 years, Kanwar shall be 3 times as old as he was 4 years ago. Find his present age.

Solution

Let Kenwar’s present age be x yr.
After 12 yr, Kanwar’s age = (x + 12)yr
and 4 yr ago, Kanwar’s age = (x - 4)yr
According to the question,
x + 12 = 3 (x - 4) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 12 = 3 x -12 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 x -x = 12 + 12 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 24
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{24}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 12
Hence, Kanwar’s present age is 12 yr.

Q 92

Anima left one-half of her property to her daughter, one-third to her son and donated the rest to an educational institute. If the donation was worth ₹ 1,00,000, how much money did Anima have?

Solution

Let Anima’s property be ₹ x.
We have given that property left for her daughter = ₹ {tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex}
Now, Property left for her son =  ₹ {tex}\frac{x}{3}{/tex}
Remaining property = {tex}\left[x-\left(\frac{x}{2}+\frac{x}{3}\right)\right]{/tex}
{tex}=x-\left(\frac{3 x+2x}{6}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{6 x-5 x}{6}{/tex}
=₹{tex}\frac{ x}{6}{/tex}
Since, the remaining property donated to an educational institute.
But donation property = ₹100000
{tex}\therefore \quad \frac{x}{6}=100000 {/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}x = 600000
Hence, Anima had ₹ 600000

Q 93

If {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} is subtracted from a number and the difference is multiplied by 4, the result is 5. What is the number?

Solution

Let the number be x.
According to the question,
{tex}4\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)=5 \quad \Rightarrow{/tex} 4x - 2 = 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x = 5 + 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x = 7
{tex}\therefore \quad x=\frac{7}{4}{/tex}
Hence, the required number is {tex}\frac{7}{4}{/tex}

Q 94

The sum of four consecutive integers is 266. What are the integers?

Solution

Let the four consecutive integers be x, x + 1, x + 2 and x + 3.
According to the given question,
x + x + 1 + x + 2 + x + 3 = 266
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x + 6 = 266
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x = 266 - 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x = 260
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{260}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 65
Therefore, the four consecutive integers are 65,65+1,65 + 2 and 65+3
i.e. 65, 66, 67 and 68

Q 95

Hamid has three boxes of different fruits. Box A weighs {tex}2 \frac{1}{2}{/tex} kg more than Box B and Box C weighs {tex}10 \frac{1}{4}{/tex} kg more than Box B. The total weight of the three boxes is {tex}48 \frac{3}{4}{/tex} kg. How many kilograms does Box A weigh?

Solution

Let the weight of box A be x kg.
We have given that,
Weight of Box A = Weight of Box B + {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex} kg
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Weight of box B = {tex}\left(x-\frac{5}{2}\right){/tex}kg
and weight of Box C = Weight of box B +  {tex}\frac{41}{4}{/tex} kg
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Weight of Box C = {tex}x-\frac{5}{2}+\frac{41}{4} \mathrm{kg}=x+\left(\frac{-10+41}{4}\right) \mathrm{kg}=\left(x+\frac{31}{4}\right) \mathrm{kg}{/tex}
As, total weight of three boxes = {tex}48 \frac{3}{4} \mathrm{kg}=\frac{195}{4} \mathrm{kg}{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} Total weight of three boxes = Weight of box A + Weight of box B + Weight of box C
{tex}\therefore \quad \frac{195}{4}=x+x-\frac{5}{2}+x+\frac{31}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{195}{4}=3 x+\frac{31-10}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{195}{4}=3 x+\frac{21}{4} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 3 x=\frac{195}{4}-\frac{21}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 3 x=\frac{174}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{174}{3 \times 4} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow x=\frac{29}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad x=14 \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{kg}{/tex}
Hence, box A weighs {tex}14 \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{kg}{/tex}.

Q 96

The perimeter of a rectangle is 240 cm. If its length is increased by 10% and its breadth is decreased by 20%, then we get the same perimeter. Find the length and breadth of the rectangle.

Solution

It is given that perimeter of rectangle = 240 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} (Length + Breadth) = 240 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Length + Breadth = 120 cm
Now, Let the length of rectangle be x cm.
Then, the breadth of rectangle = (120- x) cm
Now,  the new length of rectangle = {tex}x+10 \% \text { of } x{/tex}
{tex}=x+\frac{10}{100} \times x{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{110}{100} x {/tex}
and new breadth of rectangle = (120 - x) - 20% of (120 - x)
= {tex}(120-x)-\frac{20}{100} \times(120-x){/tex}
= {tex}(120-x)\left(1-\frac{20}{100}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{80}{100}(120-x) \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
It is given that, the perimeter of the new rectangle is 240 cm.
According to the question,
2 {tex}\times{/tex} (New length + New breadth) = 240
{tex}2 \times\left[\frac{110 x}{100}+\frac{80}{100}(120-x)\right]=240{/tex}
{tex} \frac{110 x+9600-80 x}{100}=\frac{240}{2}{/tex}
{tex}30 x+9600=\frac{240}{2} \times 100{/tex}
30 x + 9600 = 12000
30x = 12000 - 9600
30x = 2400
{tex}x=\frac{2400}{30}=80{/tex}
Therefore, length of rectangle = 80 cm
and breadth of rectangle = 120 - 80= 40 cm

Q 97

The age of A is five years more than that of B. 5 years ago, the ratio of their ages was 3 : 2. Find their present ages.

Solution

Let the present age of B be x yr.
then, the present age of A = (x + 5) yr.
Five years ago, age of A = (x + 5) - 5 = x yr.
and age of B = (x - 5) yr
According to the question, {tex}\frac{x}{x-5}=\frac{3}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 3(x - 5)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 3x - 15
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x - 2x = 15
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 15
therefore, the present age of B is 15 yr.
and the present age of A is (15 + 5), i.e. 20 yr.

Q 98

If numerator is 2 less than denominator of a rational number and when 1 is subtracted from numerator and denominator both, the rational number in its simplest form is {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}. What is the rational number?

Solution

Let denominator of a rational number be x.
Numerator = Denominator - 2
= x - 2
According to the question, 
{tex}\frac{(x-2)-1}{x-1}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2[(x - 2)-1] = x - 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2(x - 3) = x -1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x - 6 = x - 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x - x = 6 -1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 5
Hence, denominator = 5
and numerator = 5 - 2
= 3
Therefore, the rational number is {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex}.

Q 99

In a two-digit number, digit in unit's place is twice the digit in ten's place. If 27 is added to it, digits are reversed. Find the number.

Solution

Let ten’s digit of two-digit number be x.
Then, unit’s digit = 2x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Number = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} x + 2x 
= 10x + 2x 
= 12x
Now, On reversing the digits,
New number = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} (2x) + x
= 20x + x
= 21x
According to the question,
12x + 27 = 21x
= 21x - 12x = 27
= 9x = 27
= x = 3
Therefore, the required number is 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 3,
i.e. 36

Q 100

A man was engaged as typist for the month of February in 2009(consider it leap year). He was paid ₹ 500 per day, but ₹100 per day were deducted for the days he remained absent. He received ₹ 9,100 as salary for the month. For how many days did he work?

Solution

Let the man worked as a typist for x days in the month of February 2009. Then, absent in the month of February 2009 = (29 - x) days
[According to ques, number of days in month of February 2009 is 29]
Per day payment = ₹500 [given]
Total paid amount for working days = ₹500x
And per day deduction, when he remained absent = ₹100 [given]
Total amount deducted for being absent = ₹100 {tex}\times{/tex} (29 - x)
Given salary received by man for the month of February is ₹9100.
According to the question,
500 x - 100 (29 - x) = 9100
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 500x - 2900 + 100x = 9100
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 600x = 9100 + 2900
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 600x = 12000
{tex}\Rightarrow x = \frac{12000}{600}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 20
Therefore, the man worked for 20 days.

Q 101

A steamer goes downstream and covers the distance between two ports in 3 hours. It covers distance in 5 hours, when it goes upstream. If the stream flows at 3 km/h, then find what is the speed of the steamer upstream?

Solution

Let the speed of the steamer in still water be x km/h.
Speed of the stream = 3 km/h [given]
Now, speed of the steamer downstream = (x + 3) km/h
and speed of the steamer upstream = (x - 3) km/h
According to the question,
distance covered in 3 h by steamer downstream = distance covered in 5 h by steamer upstream
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 3 (x + 3) = 5(x - 3) [{tex}\because{/tex} distance = speed {tex}\times{/tex} time]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 9 = 5x - 15
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x - 5x = - 15 - 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 2x = -24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = - 24 {tex}\times (\frac{-1}{2}){/tex} 
=12 km/h
Therefore, the speed of the steamer in still water = 12 km/h
thus, the required speed of the steamer upstream is (12 - 3), i.e. 9 km/h.

Q 102

A lady went to a bank with ₹ 1,00,000. She asked the cashier to give her ₹ 500 and ₹1,000 currency notes in return. She got 175 currency notes in all. Find the number of each kind of currency notes.

Solution

Given, the total number of currency notes = 175
Let the total number of notes of ₹ 500 be x.
Then, the total number of notes of ₹1000 = 175 - x
Total amount which lady had = ₹100000 [given]
According to the question,
500x + (175 - x) 1000 = 100000
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 500x + 175000 - 1000x = 100000
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 500x = 100000 -175000
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 500x = - 75000
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x=-75000 \times\left(\frac{-1}{500}\right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore \space x=150{/tex}
Hence, the total number of notes of ₹ 500 = 150
and thus, the total number of notes of ₹ 1000 = 175 - 150
= 25

Q 103

There are 40 passengers in a bus, some with ₹ 3 tickets and remaining with ₹10 tickets. The total collection from these passengers is ₹ 295. Find how many passengers have tickets worth ₹ 3?

Solution

It is given that, the total number of passengers = 40
Let the number of passengers having tickets worth ₹ 3 be x
Then, the number of passengers having tickets worth ₹10 = (40 - x)
Total collection from passengers = ₹ 295 [given]
According to the question,
3x + 10(40- x ) = 295
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 400 - 10x = 295
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 7x = 295 - 400
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -7x = -105
{tex}x=\frac{-105}{-7}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad x=15{/tex}
Hence, the required number of passengers having tickets worth ₹ 3 = 15

Q 104

Denominator of a number is 4 less than its numerator. If 6 is added to the numerator, it becomes thrice the denominator. Find the fraction.

Solution

Let the numerator of the number be x.
Then, the denominator of the number = x - 4
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Fraction = {tex}\frac{x}{x-4}{/tex} ...(i)
According to the question,
x + 6 = 3 (x - 4)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x - 12 = x + 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x - x = 6 +12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 18
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{18}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore x=9{/tex}
Hence, the required fraction {tex}=\frac{9}{9-4}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{9}{5}{/tex}

Q 105

An employee works in a company on a contract of 30 days on the condition that he will receive ₹120 for each day he works and he will be fined ₹10 for each day he is absent. If he receives ₹2300 in all, for how many days did he remain absent?

Solution

We have given that, the total number of days in contract = 30
Money received per day for working = ₹120
Money deducted per day for being absent = ₹10
and the amount received by the employee as salary = ₹2300
Let the employee remained absent for x days
Then, the employee worked for (30 - x) days.
According to the question,
120{tex}\times{/tex}(30 - x) - 10{tex}\times{/tex}x = 2300
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3600 - 120x - 10x = 2300
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -130x = - 3600 + 2300
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -130x = -1300
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}x=\frac{-1300}{-130}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad x=10{/tex}
Therefore, the employee remained absent for 10 days.

Q 106

Kusum buys some chocolates at the rate of ₹10 per chocolate. She also buys an equal number of candies at the rate of ₹5 per candy. She makes a 20% profit on chocolates and 8% profit on candies. At the end of the day, all chocolates and candies are sold out and her profit is ₹240. Find the number of chocolates purchased.

Solution

Let Kusum purchased x chocolates.
Then, the total cost of chocolates = ₹10x
Similarly, she purchased x candies.
Then, the total cost of candies = ₹5x
According to the question,
Profit on chocolates = 20% of 10x
= {tex} \frac{20}{100} \times 10 x{/tex} 
= ₹2x
and profit on candies = 8% of 5x
= {tex}\frac{8}{100} \times 5 x{/tex} 
= ₹0.4x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total profit = 2x + 0.4x
= ₹2.4x
Again, according to the question,
2.4x = 240  
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{240}{2.4}{/tex} 
= 100
Therefore, she purchased 100 chocolates.

Q 107

A steamer goes downstream and covers the distance between two ports in 5 hours, while it covers the same distance upstream in 6 hours. If the speed of the stream is 1 km/h, then find the speed of the steamer in still water.

Solution

Given, the speed of stream = 1 km/h
Let the speed of the steamer in still water be x km/h.
Then, the speed of steamer downstream = (x + 1) km/h
and the speed of steamer upstream = (x - 1) km/h
According to the question,
Distance covered upstream = Distance covered downstream
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 1) {tex} \times{/tex} 6 = (x + 1) {tex}\times{/tex} 5 [{tex}\because{/tex} distance = speed {tex}\times{/tex} time]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x - 6 = 5x + 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x - 5x = 6 + 5
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 11
Hence, the required speed of steamer in still water is 11 km/h.

Q 108

Distance between two places A and B is 210 km. Two cars start simultaneously from A and B in opposite directions and distance between them after 3 hours is 54 km. If speed of one car is less than that of other by 8 km/h, then find the speed of each.

Solution

Let the speed of car starts from A be x km/h.
Then, speed of car starts from B = (x + 8) km/h
Given, distance between the cars after 3 h = 54 km
According to the question,

Total distance covered by both cars in 3 h = 54 km
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 210 - [3x + 3(x + 8)] = 54[{tex}\because{/tex} distance = speed {tex} \times{/tex} time]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 210 - 3x - 3x - 24 = 54
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 6x = - 210 + 24 + 54
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 6x = - 210 + 78
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - 6x = - 132
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{-132}{-6}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 22
Hence, the speed of car starts from A = 22 km/h
and the speed of car starts from B = 22 + 8 = 30 km/h

Q 109

A carpenter charged ₹2500 for making a bed. The cost of materials used is ₹1100 and the labour charge is ₹200 per hour. For how many hours did the carpenter work?

Solution

Let the carpenter worked for x h.
Given, labour charge = ₹200 per hour
Then, total labour charge = ₹200x
As, amount charged by carpenter for making a bed = Cost of materials + Total labour charge
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2500 = 1100 + 200x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 200x = 2500 -1100
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 200 x=1400 \Rightarrow x=\frac{1400}{200}=7{/tex}
Hence, the carpenter worked for 7 h.

Q 110

For what value of x, the perimeter of shape will be 77 cm?

Solution

Given, the perimeter of shape = 77 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Sum of all sides of the shape = 77
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x + 1) + (x + 1) + (x + 2) + (2x + 1) + (2x + 2) =77
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7x + 7 = 77 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7x = 77 - 7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7x = 70 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{70}{7}=10{/tex}
Hence, the value of x is 10 cm.

Q 111

For what value of x is the perimeter of shape 186 cm?

Solution

Given, length of the given figure = (5x + 6)
and breadth of the given figure = (2x + 66)
{tex}\because{/tex} Perimeter of shape = 186 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2  (Length + Breadth) = 186
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 {tex} \times{/tex} [(5x + 6) + (2x + 66)] = 186
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 {tex} \times{/tex} [7x + 72] = 186
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 14x + 144 = 186
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 14x = 186 -144
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 14x = 42
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{42}{14}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 3
Hence, the value of x is 3 cm.

Q 112

On dividing ₹200 between A and B such that twice of A's share is less than 3 times B's share by 200. what is B's share ?

Solution

Let B's share Rs x
So, A's share = ₹(200 - x)
Now, according to the question.
2(200 - x) = 3x - 200
400 - 2x = 3x - 200
3x + 2x = 400 + 200
5x = 600
{tex}\therefore x = \frac{{600}}{5} = 120{/tex}
x = 120
So, B's share is ₹120
A's share is ₹80

Q 113

Madhulika thought of a number, doubled it and added 20 to it. On dividing the resulting number by 25, she gets 4. What is the number?

Solution

Let the number be x.
According to the question, {tex}\frac{2 x+20}{25}=4{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x + 20 = 25 {tex} \times{/tex} 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x + 20 = 100
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x= 100-20
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 80
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{80}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 40
Hence, the required number is 40.

Q 24

The sides AB and CD of a quadrilateral ABCD are extended to points P and Q respectively. Is {tex}\angle{/tex}ADQ + {tex}\angle{/tex}CBP = {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}C? Give reason.

Solution

At first join AC, then
{tex}\angle{/tex}CBP = {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC...(i) [Exterior angles of {tex}\Delta{/tex} ABC]
and {tex}\angle{/tex}ADQ = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD + {tex}\angle{/tex}DAC...(ii) [Exterior angles of {tex}\Delta{/tex} ADC]

Add eqn. (i) and eqn. (ii)
Therefore, {tex}\angle{/tex}CBP + {tex}\angle{/tex}ADQ = {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD + {tex}\angle{/tex}DAC
= ({tex}\angle{/tex}BCA + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD) + ({tex}\angle{/tex}BAC + {tex}\angle{/tex}DAC)
= {tex}\angle{/tex}C + {tex}\angle{/tex}A
Hence proved.

Q 25

If AM and CN are perpendiculars on the diagonal BD of a parallelogram ABCD, Is {tex}\triangle{/tex}AMD {tex}\cong{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}CNB? Give reason.

Solution

The following figure can be drawn according to given information:

In {tex}\Delta{/tex} AMD and {tex}\Delta{/tex}CNB,
AD = BC (opposite sides of the parallelogram)
{tex}\angle{/tex}AMB = {tex}\angle{/tex}CNB [Each 90o]
{tex}\angle{/tex}ADM = {tex}\angle{/tex}NBC [AD || BC and BD is transversal]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}AMD {tex}\cong{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}CNB [AAS congruent criteron]

Q 26

Find x in the following figure.

Solution

From the given figure it is clear that
 {tex}\angle{/tex}1 + 90o = 180o (linear pair)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}1 = 90o
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}A +{tex}\angle{/tex}B +{tex}\angle{/tex}C +{tex}\angle{/tex}D + {tex}\angle{/tex}E = 360o[Sum of exterior angles of a polygon = 360o]
90o + 40o + x + 60o + 90o = 360o
x + 280o = 360o
x = 80o

Q 27

Two adjacent angles of a parallelogram are in the ratio 4 : 5. Find their measures.

Solution

Let the angles be 4x and 5x
Then, 4x + 5x = 180o[Sum of co-interior angles = 1800]
9x = 180o
x = 20o
So, angles are 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 20o = 80o 
and 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 20o = 100o

Q 28

The four angles of a quadrilateral are in the ratio 3 : 4 : 5 : 6. Find the angles.

Solution

Let angles be 3x, 4x, 5x, 6x. 
Thus, 3x + 4x + 5x + 6x = 360°
 since sum of the angles of a quadrilateral is 360°. 
So, 18x = 360° or, x = 20° 
Thus, angles are 60°, 80°, 100°, 120°.

Q 29

In a parallelogram PQRS, the bisectors of {tex}\angle{/tex}P and {tex}\angle{/tex}Q meet at O. Find {tex}\angle{/tex}POQ

Solution

It is given that OP and OQ are the bisectors of {tex}\angle{/tex}P and {tex}\angle{/tex}Q respectively (see figure on the right),

so, {tex}\angle{/tex}OPQ = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}P and {tex}\angle{/tex}OQP = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}Q
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}POQ
{tex}\angle{/tex}OPQ + {tex}\angle{/tex}PQO + {tex}\angle{/tex}POQ = 180o (Angle sum property)
i.e. {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}P + {tex}\angle{/tex}POQ + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}Q = 180o
i.e. {tex}\angle{/tex}POQ = 180o - {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} ({tex}\angle{/tex}P + {tex}\angle{/tex}Q)[{tex}\because{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}P + {tex}\angle{/tex}Q = 180o ,Co-interior angles]
= 180o - {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 180o
= 90o

Q 30

Three angles of a quadrilateral are 50o, 40o and 123o. Find its fourth angle.

Solution

Let fourth angle be x.
Then, 50o + 40o + 123o + x = 360o[Sum of interior angles of quadrilateral = 3600]
or x = 360o - 50o - 40o - 123o
x = 360o - 213o 
x = 147o 

Q 31

The ratio of exterior angle to interior angle of a regular polygon is 1 : 4. Find the number of sides of the polygon.

Solution

Let the exterior angle of the polygon be x
Then, the interior angle of polygon = 180o - x [{tex}\because{/tex}Sum of  interior angle and exterior angle = 1800]
According to question,
{tex}\frac{x}{180^{\circ}-x}=\frac{1}{4}{/tex}
or, 4x = 180o - x
or, 4x = 180o - x
or, 5x = 180o
or, x = {tex}\frac{180^{\circ}}{5}{/tex}
So, x = 36o
Number of sides of polygon = {tex}\frac{360^{\circ}}{\text { exterior angle }}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{360^{\circ}}{36^{\circ}}{/tex}
= 10

Q 32

Each interior angle of a polygon is 108o. Find the number of sides of the polygon.

Solution

Since, interior angle = 108o
so, exterior angle = 180o - 108o [{tex}\because{/tex} Sum of interior angle and exterior angle = 1800]
= 72o
Number of sides = {tex}\frac{360^{\circ}}{\text { exterior angle }}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{360^{\circ}}{72^{\circ}}{/tex}
= 5

Q 33

Construct a rhombus PAIR, given that PA = 6 cm and {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 110°.

Solution

We know that Rhombus has all sides equal.
Steps of construction: 

  1. Draw AI = 6 cm
  2. Draw ray AX such that {tex}\angle{/tex}IAX = 110° and draw IY such that {tex}\angle{/tex}AIY = 70°[Adjacent angles of rhombus are supplementary]
  3. With A and I as centres and radius 6 cm draw arcs intersecting AX and IY at P and R respectively.
  4. Join PR.
  5. Thus, PAIR is the required rhombus.
Q 34

One of the diagonals of a rhombus and its sides are equal. Find the angles of the rhombus.

Solution

It is given that PQRS is a rhombus such that its diagonal PR is equal to its sides, i.e. PQ = QR = RS = PS = PR So, {tex}\triangle{/tex}PRS and {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR are equilateral.

{tex}\angle{/tex}S = {tex}\angle{/tex}Q = 60o [{tex}\because{/tex}Each angle of an equilateral triangle is 60o]
and {tex}\angle{/tex}P = {tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2
= 60o + 60o 
= 120o 
{tex}\angle{/tex}R =  {tex}\angle{/tex}P = 120o[Opposite angles of parallelogram]
Hence {tex}\angle{/tex}S = {tex}\angle{/tex}Q = 60o 
and {tex}\angle{/tex}P = {tex}\angle{/tex}R = 120o

Q 35

In the figure, HOPE is a rectangle. Its diagonals meet at G. If HG = 5x + 1 and EG = 4x + 19, find x.

Solution

Since diagonals of a rectangle bisect each other, HP = 2HG
= 2 (5x + 1) 
= 10x + 2
and OE = 2EG = 2(4x +19) = 8x + 38 
Diagonals of a rectangle are equal. 
So HP = OE  
or 10x + 2 = 8x + 38 
or  2x = 36 
or x = 18

Q 36

Application on the problem strategy:
RICE is a rhombus. Find x, y, z. Justify your findings. Also, find the perimeter of the rhombus.

Solution

Understand and explore the problem:
We have to find the values of x, y, z. i.e. OE, OY and side IR of the rhombus and perimeter of the rhombus.
Since, RICE is a rhombus and OC = 12, OE = 5, OI = x + 2, OR = x + y, ER = z
Plan a strategy

  1. We have to find the parts of the diagonal. Use diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other.
  2. We have to find the side of the rhombus. We use diagonals intersect at right angles and apply Pythagoras theorem.
  3. Since all sides of a rhombus are equal, therefore, the perimeter of the rhombus = 4 × side.

Solve
Step 1. OI = OE {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 2 = 5 or x = 5 - 2 = 3.
OC = OR {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12 = y + x or y = 12 - x
12 - 3 = 9
Step 2. {tex}\Delta{/tex}EOR is a right triangle
{tex}\therefore{/tex}ER2 = OE2 + OR2
= 52 + 122
= 25 + 144 = 169
ER = {tex}\sqrt{169}{/tex} = 13 cm
or z = ER = 13 cm
Step 3. Since all sides of a rhombus are equal
{tex}\therefore{/tex} RE = RI = IC = CE = 13 cm
Perimeter of RICE = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} RE = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 13 cm
= 52 cm
Revise
We have been asked to find x, y and z and we have found that.
Checking
x + 2 = 5 and x = 3 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 + 2 = 5
Hence value of x is correct.
x + y = 12 {tex}\because{/tex} x = 3 and y = 9
and 3 + 9 = 12 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} value of y is correct.
Perimeter of rhombus = {tex}2 \sqrt{\mathrm{d} _1^{2}+\mathrm{d} _2^{2}}{/tex} (where d1 and d2 are diagonals)
{tex}=2 \sqrt{24^{2}+10^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=2 \sqrt{576+100}{/tex}
{tex}=2 \sqrt{676}{/tex} = 52 cm
or perimeter of rhombus = 4{tex}\times{/tex}13 = 52 cm
