Q 60

Using prime factorisation, check whether 729 is perfect square or not.

Solution


Prime factors of 729 = (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) {tex}\times{/tex} (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) {tex}\times{/tex} (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3)
As after grouping, there is no unpaired factor left over.
So, 729 is a perfect square.

Q 61

Using distributive law, find the square of 101.

Solution

By using distributive law,
We have, 101 = 100 + 1
So, 1012 = (100 + 1)2
= (100 + 1) (100 + 1)
= 100 (100 + 1) + 1 (100 + 1)
= ((100 {tex}\times{/tex} 100) + (100 {tex}\times{/tex} 1)) + ((1 {tex}\times{/tex} 100) + (1 {tex}\times{/tex} 1))
= 10000 + 100 + 100 + 1
= 10201
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The square of the given number i.e. 1012 = 10201

Q 62

Using distributive law, find the square of 72.

Solution

We have, 722 = 72 {tex}\times{/tex} 72 = 72 {tex}\times{/tex} (70 + 2) = 5040 + 144 = 5184

Q 63

Can a right-angled triangle with sides 6 cm, 10 cm, and 8 cm be formed? Give reason.

Solution

We know that the sum of the squares of two smaller sides is equal to the square of the longer side.
Because, 102 = 100 = 64 + 36 = 82 + 62
Hence, 6 cm, 8 cm and 10 cm are the sides of a right angled triangle.

Q 64

Write the Pythagorean triplet whose one of the numbers is 4.

Solution

We know that for any natural number greater than 1, (2m, m2 -1, m2 + 1) is a Pythagorean triplet.
So, one number is 2m, then the other two numbers are m2+1 and m2 - 1.
Hence, one number is 4, then Pythagorean triplet, 2m = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} m = 2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} m2 + 1 = 22 + 1 = 4 + 1 = 5
and m2 - 1 = 22 - 1 = 4 - 1 = 3
Now, 32 + 42 = 52
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9 + 16 = 25 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 25 = 25
So, 3, 4 and 5 are Pythagorean triplets.

Q 65

Using prime factorization, find the square root of 11025.

Solution

We have, 11025

Now, 11025 = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 7
Therefore, Square root of 11025 = {tex}\sqrt{11025}=3 \times 5 \times 7{/tex} =105

Q 66

Using prime factorisation, find the square root of 4761

Solution

We have, 4761

Now, 4761 = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 23 {tex}\times{/tex} 23
Therefore, Square root of 4761 = {tex}\sqrt{4761}=3 \times 23{/tex} = 69

Q 67

Is 176 a perfect square? If not, find the smallest number by which it should be multiplied to get a perfect square.

Solution

Prime factors of 176 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 11 Here, 11 has no pair.
So, it is not a perfect square and 11 is the smallest number by which 176 should be multiplied to get a perfect square.
Then, 176 {tex}\times{/tex} 11 = 22 {tex}\times{/tex} 22 {tex}\times{/tex} 11 {tex}\times{/tex} 11
= 22 {tex}\times{/tex} 22 {tex}\times{/tex} 112
= 1936 = (44)2
which is a perfect square of 44.
Hence, the smallest number is 11 by which it should be multiplied to get a perfect square.

Q 68

Write two Pythagorean triplets each having one of the numbers as 5.

Solution

As, 52 = 32 + 42
and 132 = 122 + 52
Hence, 3, 4, 5 and 12, 5, 13 are the pythagorean triplets.

Q 69

By what smallest number should 216 be divided so that the quotient is a perfect square? Also, find the square root of the quotient.

Solution

Prime factors of 216 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
Grouping the factors into pairs of equal factors, we get
216 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
We find that there is no prime factor to form a pair with 2 and 3.
Therefore, we must divide the number by 6, so that the quotient becomes a perfect square.
If we divide the given number by 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 i.e.6, then
New number = {tex}\frac{216}{6}{/tex} = 36
Taking one factor from each, we get square root of new number (quotient) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 6

Q 70

Find the square root of 1369 by Division method.

Solution


Therefore, {tex}\sqrt {1369} {/tex}=37

Q 71

Find the square root of 5625 by long division method.

Solution

We have, square root of 5625 by long division method,

{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{5625}{/tex} = 75

Q 72

Find the square root of 27.04 by long division method.

Solution

The square root of 27.04 by long division method:

{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{27.04}{/tex} = 5.2 

Q 73

Find the square root of 1.44 by the long division method.

Solution

We have, 1.44

{tex}\therefore \quad \sqrt{1.44}=1.2{/tex}

Q 74

What is the least number, that should be subtracted from 1385 to get a perfect square? Also, find the square root of the perfect square.

Solution

First, find the square root of 1385 by long division method.

Hence, the least number is 16, which should be subtracted from 1385 to get a perfect square and the required perfect square number = 1385 - 16 = 1369
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Square root of 1369 = {tex}\sqrt{37 \times 37}{/tex} = 37

Q 75

What is the least number that should be added to 6200 to make it a perfect square?

Solution

First, find the square root of 6200 by long division method.

Hence, the least number is 41, which should be added to 6200 to make it a perfect square and the perfect square number = 6200 + 41 = 6241
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Square root of 6241 = {tex}\sqrt{79 \times 79}{/tex} = 79

Q 76

Find the least number of four digits that is a perfect square.

Solution

Since, the least number of four digits is 1000.
Now, find the square root of 1000 by long division method.

Hence, the least number of four digits, i.e. a perfect square = 1000 + 24 = 1024

Q 77

Find the greatest number of three digits that is a perfect square.

Solution

Since, the greatest number of three digits is 1000. Now, find the square root of 1000 by long division method.

Hence, the greatest number of three digits, i.e. a perfect square = 1000 - 39= 961

Q 78

Find the least square number, which is exactly divisible by 3, 4, 5, 6 and 8.

Solution

The least square number divisible by each of 3, 4, 5, 6 and 8, is equal to the LCM of 3, 4, 5, 6 and 8.

{tex}\therefore{/tex} LCM of 3, 4, 5, 6 and 8 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex}2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 120
The prime factorisation of 120 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
Here, prime factors 2, 3 and 5 are unpaired. Clearly, to make it a perfect square, it must be multiplied by 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5, i.e. 30.
Therefore, required number = 120 {tex}\times{/tex} 30= 3600
Hence, the least square number is 3600.

Q 79

Find the length of the side of a square, if the length of its diagonal is 10 cm.

Solution

Given, length of diagonal = 10 cm Suppose, the length of side of a square is x cm . By using Pythagoras theorem,

(10)2 = x2 + x2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 100 = 2x2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  x2 = 50 
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\sqrt{50}{/tex} [taking square root on both sides]
{tex}\therefore \quad x=5 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
Hence, the length of the side of square is {tex}\sqrt{50} \text { or } 5 \sqrt{2}{/tex} cm.

Q 80

A decimal number is multiplied by itself. If the product is 51.84, then find the number.

Solution

Let the number be x. Then, product = x {tex}\times{/tex} x = x2
But product =51.84 [given]
therefore, x2 = 51.84
{tex}\Rightarrow x = \sqrt{51.84}{/tex}
Now, place the bar over the numbers, then square root is given below,
{tex}\therefore x = \sqrt{51.84} = 7.2{/tex}

Hence, the required number is 7.2

Q 81

Find the decimal fraction, which when multiplied by itself, gives 84.64.

Solution

Let the decimal fraction be x.
When a decimal fraction is multiplied by itself, then product = x {tex}\times{/tex} x = x2
But product = 84.64 [given]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x2 = 84.64
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\sqrt{84.64}{/tex}
Now, place the bar over the numbers, then the square root is given below.

{tex}\therefore \quad x=\sqrt{84.64}=9.2{/tex}
Hence, the required decimal is 9.2

Q 82

A farmer wants to plough his square field of side 150 m. How much area will he have to plough?

Solution

Given, side of square field = 150 m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of square field = Side {tex}\times{/tex} Side = 150 {tex}\times{/tex} 150 = 22500 m2

Q 83

What will be the number of unit squares on each side of a square graph paper, if the total number of unit squares is 256?

Solution

Let the number be x. Then, number of unit squares = x {tex}\times{/tex} x = x2 
But total number of unit squares = 256
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x2 = 256
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\sqrt{256}=\sqrt{16 \times 16}{/tex} = 16
Hence, the required number of squares is 16.

Q 84

The dimensions of a rectangular field are 80 m and 18 m. Find the length of its diagonal.

Solution

Here, length of a rectangular field (l) = 80 m
and breadth of a rectangular field (b) = 18 m

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Length of diagonal = {tex}\sqrt{l^{2}+b^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{(80)^{2}+(18)^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{6400+324}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{6724}{/tex} = 82 m

Q 85

Find the area of a square field, if its perimeter is 96 m.

Solution

Given, perimeter of square field = 96 m
{tex}\because{/tex} Perimeter of square = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} Side
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} (Side) = 96
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Side = 24 m
{tex}\therefore{/tex}Area of square field = (Side)2 = (24)2 = 576 m2

Q 86

Three numbers are in the ratio 1:2:3 and the sum of their cubes is 4500. Find the numbers.

Solution

Let the three numbers be x, 2x and 3x.
According to the question,
(x)3 + (2x)3 + (3x)3 = 4500
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x3 + 8x3 + 27x3 = 4500
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 36x3 = 4500
{tex}\Rightarrow x^{3}=\frac{4500}{36}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x3 = 125
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\sqrt[3]{125}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\sqrt[3]{5 \times 5 \times 5}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 5
Hence, the numbers are 5, 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 and 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5, i.e. 5, 10 and 15

Q 87

How many square meters of carpet will be required for a square room of side 6.5 m to be carpeted?

Solution

Given, side of a square room = 6.5 m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of carpet to be required = (Side of square room)2
= (6.5)2 = 6.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 6.5 = 42.25 m2

Q 88

Find the side of a square, whose area is equal to the area of a rectangle with sides 6.4 m and 2.5 m.

Solution

Given dimensions of a rectangle are 6.4 m and 2.5 m. Area of a rectangle = Length {tex}\times{/tex} Breadth = 6.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5= 16 m2
Let the side of square be xm.
According to the question,
Area of square = Area of rectangle
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = 16
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\sqrt{16}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\sqrt{4 \times 4}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 4 m
Hence, the side of a square is 4 m.

Q 89

Find the number of plants in each row, if 1024 plants are arranged, so that number of plants in a row is the same as the number of rows.

Solution

Let the number of plants in each row be x.
Then, number of rows = number of plants in each row = x

Total plants = x {tex}\times{/tex} x = x2
According to the question, x2 = 1024
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\sqrt{1024}=\sqrt{32 \times 32}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 32 Hence, there are 32 plants in each row.

Q 90

A hall has a capacity of 2704 seats. If the number of rows is equal to the number of seats in each row, then find the number of seats in each row.

Solution

Let the number of seats in each row be x.
Then, number of rows = Number of seats in each row = x
Total seats = x {tex}\times{/tex} x = x2
According to the question, x2 =2704

{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\sqrt{2704}=\sqrt{52 \times 52}{/tex}
= x = 52
Hence, there are 52 seats in each row.

Q 91

A General wishes to draw up his 7500 soldiers in the form of a square. After arranging, he found out that some of them are left out. How many soldiers were left out?

Solution

Given, total number of soldiers = 7500

Hence, 104 soldiers were left out.

Q 92

8649 students were sitting in a lecture room in such a manner that there were as many students in the row as there were rows in the lecture room. How many students were there in each row of the lecture room?

Solution

Let number of students in each row of the lecture room be x.
Then, number of rows = x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total students =  x  {tex}\times{/tex} x = x2
According to the question,
x2 = 8649
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\sqrt{8649}{/tex}


 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 93
Hence, there are 93 students in each row of the lecture room.

Q 93

Rahul walks 12 m North from his house and turns West to walk 35 m to reach his friend's house. While returning, he walks diagonally from his friend's house to reach back to his house. What distance did he walk, while returning?

Solution

Let Rahul walked x m, while returning home.

In {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABC, by using Pythagoras theorem, we get

AC2 = AB2 + BC2
= AC2 = (12)2 + (35)2
= AC2 = 144 + 1225 = 1369
= AC = {tex}\sqrt{1369}{/tex}
= AC = 37 m
Hence, Rahul walked 37 m distance for returning to his house diagonally.

Q 94

A 5.5 m long ladder is leaned against a wall. The ladder reaches the wall to a height of 4.4 m. Find the distance between the wall and the foot of the ladder.

Solution

Let the distance between the wall and the foot of the ladder be xm

In right-angled {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABC, by using Pythagoras theorem, we get
BC2 = AB2 + AC2
= (5.5)2 = x2 + (4.4)2
= x2 = (5.5)2 - (4.4)2
= x2 = 30.25 - 19.36
= x2 = 10.89
= AC = {tex}\sqrt{10.89}{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} AC = 3.3 m

Hence, the distance between the wall and the foot of the ladder is 3.3 m.

Q 95

A king wanted to reward his advisor, a Wiseman of the kingdom. So, he asked Wiseman to name his own reward. The Wiseman thanked the king but said that he would ask only for some gold coins each day for a month. The coins were to be counted out in a pattern of one coin for the first day, 3 coins for the second day, 5 coins for the third day and so on for 30 days. Without making calculations, find how many coins will the advisor get in that month?

Solution

According to the question, the advisor gets coins up to 30 days in the pattern given as: 1+ 3 + 5 + ...
Here, n = 30
We know that, The sum of first n odd natural numbers = n2
= (30)2
= 900
Hence, the advisor gets 900 coins in that month.

Q 96

Find three numbers in the ratio 2 : 3 : 5, the sum of whose squares is 608.

Solution

Let the three numbers be 2x, 3x and 5x, respectively.
According to the question,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x)2 + (3x)2 + (5x)2 = 608 [given]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x2 + 9x2 + 25x2 = 608
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 38x2 = 608
{tex}\Rightarrow x^{2}=\frac{608}{38}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 =16 = (4)2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 4
Hence, the numbers are 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 4, 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 4, 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 4, i.e. 8, 12 and 20.

Q 97

Find the smallest square number divisible by each of the numbers 8, 9 and 10.

Solution

The least number divisible by each of the numbers 8, 9 and 10 is equal to the LCM of 8, 9 and 10.

{tex}\therefore{/tex} LCM of 8, 9 and 10 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 360
Prime factors of 360 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex}(3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) {tex}\times{/tex} 5
Here, prime factors 2 and 5 are unpaired. Clearly, to make it a perfect square, it must be multiplied by 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5, i.e. 10
Therefore, required number = 360 {tex}\times{/tex}10 = 3600
Hence, the smallest square number divisible by each of the numbers 8, 9 and 10 is 3600

Q 98

The area of a square plot is 101 {tex}\frac{1}{400} \mathrm{m}^{2}{/tex}. Find the length of one side of the plot.

Solution

Let the length of the square plot be a. Then, the area of square = a2
According to the question,
Area = 101 {tex}\frac{1}{400} \mathrm{m}^{2}{/tex} [given]
{tex}\therefore \quad a^{2}=101 \frac{1}{400} \Rightarrow a^{2}=\frac{40401}{400}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad a=\sqrt{\frac{40401}{400}} \Rightarrow a=\sqrt{\frac{201 \times 201}{20 \times 20}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad a=\frac{201}{20}=10 \frac{1}{20} \mathrm{m}{/tex} [taking square root on both sides]
Hence, the length of one side of the plot is {tex}10 \frac{1}{20} m{/tex}.

Q 99

Find the square root of 324 by the method of repeated subtraction.

Solution

The given number is 324.
Now, we subtract successive odd numbers starting from 1 as follows:
Here, 324 - 1 = 323, 323 - 3 = 320
320 - 5 = 315, 315 - 7 = 308
308 - 9 = 299, 299 -11 = 288
288 - 13 = 275, 275 - 15 = 260
260 - 17 = 243, 243 - 19 = 224
224 - 21 = 203, 203 - 23 = 180
180 - 25 = 155, 155 - 27 = 128
128 - 29 = 99, 99 - 31 = 68
68 - 33 = 35, 35 - 35 = 0
We observe that the number 324 reduced to zero (0) after subracting 18 odd numbers
So, 324 is a perfect square of 18
{tex}\therefore \quad \sqrt{324}=18{/tex}
Hence, the square root of 324 is 18

Q 100

Three numbers are in the ratio 2 : 3 : 4. The sum of their cubes is 0.334125. Find the numbers.

Solution

Let the numbers be 2x, 3x and 4x, respectively.
{tex}\because{/tex} Sum of their cubes = 0.334125 [given]
According to the question,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x)3 + (3x)3 + (4x)3 = 0.334125
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8x3 + 27x3 + 64x3 = 0.334125
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 99x3 = 0.334125
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x^{3}=\frac{0.334125}{99}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x3 = 0.003375
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x^{3}=\frac{3375}{1000000}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\sqrt[3]{\frac{15 \times 15 \times 15}{10 \times 10 \times 10 \times 10 \times 10 \times 10}}{/tex} [taking cube root on both sides]
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{15}{10 \times 10 \times 10}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 0.015
Hence, the required numbers are 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.015, 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.015 and 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.015, i.e. 0.03, 0.045 and 0.06.

Q 101

A perfect square number has four digits, none of which is zero. The digits from left to right have values, that are even, even, odd, even. Find the number.

Solution

Suppose abcd is a perfect square,
where, a = even number
b = even number
c = odd number
d = even number
Hence, 8836 is one of the number which satisfies the given condition.

Q 102

Put three different numbers in the circles, so that when you add the numbers at the end of each line, you always get a perfect square.

Solution

{tex}\because{/tex} 6 + 19 = 25 (perfect square)
19 + 30 = 49 (perfect square
and 30 + 6 = 36 (perfect square)

Q 103

The perimeters of two squares are 40 m and 96 m, respectively. Find the perimeter of another square equal in area to the sum of the first two squares.

Solution

Let the side of two squares be a1 and a2.
Then, perimeter of first square = 4a1
Given, perimeter of first square = 40
According to the question, 4a1 = 40 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a1 = 10
Similarly, perimeter of second square = 4a2
Given, perimeter of second square = 96
According to the question, 4a2 = 96 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 = 24
Let a be the side of another square.
Then, area of another square = a2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of another square = Sum of the areas of first and second squares
= Area of first square + Area of second square
{tex}=a_{1}^{2}+a_{2}^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 = (10)2 + (24)2 = 100 + 576 = 676 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = {tex}\sqrt{676}{/tex} = 26 m [taking square root on both sides]
Perimeter of another square = 4a = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 26 = 104 m
So, the perimeter of another square is 104 m.

Q 104

A three-digit perfect square is such that, if it is viewed upside down, the number seen is also a perfect square. What is the number?
(Hint: The digits 1, 0, and 8 stay the same when viewed upside down, whereas 9 becomes 6 and 6 becomes 9.)

Solution

Three-digit perfect squares are 196 and 961, which looks the same when viewed upside down.

Q 105

13 and 31 is a strange pair of numbers, such that their squares 169 and 961 are also mirror of each other. Can you find two other such pairs?

Solution
  1. Let a pair of numbers be 12 and 21.
    Also, (12)2 = 144 and (21)2 = 441
  2. Let a pair of numbers be 102 and 201
    Also, (102)2 =10404 and (201)2 = 40401

Q 6

Solution

= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
= 18

Write cubes of the first three multiples of 3.

Solution

Since, the first three multiples of 3 are 3, 6 and 9.
Hence, the cubes of first three multiples of 3 are (3)3, (6)3 and (9)3, i.e. 27, 216 and 729.

Q 34

Using prime factorisation, find that 128 is a perfect cube.

Solution

We have, 128 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2
Since one 2 remains after grouping in triplets.
So, 128 is not a perfect cube.

Q 35

Using prime factorization, find that 343 is a perfect cube.

Solution

We have, 343 = 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 Since the prime factors appear in triplets.
So, 343 is a perfect cube.

Q 36

Using prime factorization, show that 729 is a perfect cube.

Solution

We have, 729 = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
Since the prime factors appear in triplets.
So, 729 is a perfect cube.

Q 37

Using prime factorisation, show that 1331 is a perfect cube.

Solution

We have, 1331 = 11 {tex}\times{/tex} 11 {tex}\times{/tex} 11
Since, the prime factors appear in triplets.
So, 1331 is a perfect cube.

Q 38

Using prime factorisation, find the cube root of 512.

Solution

We have, 512

Now, 512 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 (making triplets)
{tex}\therefore \quad \sqrt[3]{512}=2 \times 2 \times 2{/tex} = 8

Q 39

Using prime factorisation, find the cube root of 2197.

Solution

We have, 2197

Now, 2197 = 13 {tex}\times{/tex} 13 {tex}\times{/tex} 13
{tex}\therefore \quad \sqrt[3]{2197}=13{/tex}

Q 40

Is 9720 a perfect cube? If not, find the smallest number by which it should be divided to get a perfect cube. Also find the cube root of the quotient.

Solution

No, 9720 is not a perfect cube. By prime factorization, we have,
9720 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 23 {tex}\times{/tex} 33 {tex}\times{/tex} 32 {tex}\times{/tex} 51
As 32 and 5 does not appear in triplet, 9720 is not a perfect cube.
To make it a perfect cube, 9720 should be divided by 32 {tex}\times{/tex} 51 = 45
Therefore it becomes, 9720 {tex}\div{/tex} 45 = 216, which is a perfect cube.
216 = 23 {tex}\times{/tex} 33 = 63
i.e., {tex}\sqrt[3]{{216}}{/tex} = 6

Q 41

By what smallest number should 3600 be multiplied so that the quotient is a perfect cube. Also, find the cube root of the quotient.

Solution

Prime factors of 3600 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 
Grouping the factors into triplets of equal factors, we get
3600 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 

We know that, if a number is to be a perfect cube, then each of its prime factors must occur thrice.
We find that 2 occurs once 3 and 5 occurs twice only.
Hence, the smallest number, by which the given number must be multiplied in order that the product is a perfect cube = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 60
Also, product = 3600 {tex}\times{/tex} 60 = 216000
Now, arranging into triplets of equal prime factors, we have
216000 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
Taking one factor from each triplets, we get
{tex}\sqrt[3]{216000}{/tex} = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 60

Q 42

If one side of a cube is 15 m in length, then find its volume.

Solution

Given, one side of a cube = 15 m
therefore, Volume of cube = (Side)3 = (15)3 = 3375 m3
Hence, the volume of cube is 3375 m3.

Q 43

Find the length of each side of a cube, if its volume is 512 cm3.

Solution

Given, volume of a cube = 512 cm3 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (Side of a cube)3 = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = (8)3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Side of a cube = 8 cm
Hence, the length of each side of the cube is 8 cm.

Q 44

The difference of two perfect cubes is 189. If the cube root of the smaller of the two numbers is 3, find the cube root of the larger number.

Solution

Given the difference of two perfect cubes =189.
Let the smaller cube number be x.
Then the other cube (larger cube) = 189 + x
Given cube root of the smaller cube = 3
i.e., {tex}\sqrt[3]{x} = 3{/tex}
Therefore, x = 33 = 27.
Larger cube = 189 + 27 = 216
Cube root of the larger cube number = {tex}\sqrt[3]{{216}}{/tex} = 6.

Q 45

Evaluate : {tex}\sqrt[3]{27}+\sqrt[3]{0.008}+\sqrt[3]{0.064}{/tex}

Solution

We have, {tex}\sqrt[3]{27}+\sqrt[3]{0.008}+\sqrt[3]{0.064}{/tex} 
= {tex}(27)^{1 / 3}+(0.008)^{1 / 3}+(0.064)^{1 / 3}{/tex} 
= {tex}\left(3 \times 3 \times 3) ^{\frac{-1}{3}}\right.{/tex} + {tex}(0.2 \times 0.2 \times 0.2)^{\frac{1}{3}}+(0.4 \times 0.4 \times 0.4)^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex} 
= {tex}(3)^{3 \times \frac{1}{3}}+(0.2)^{3 \times \frac{1}{3}}+(0.4)^{3 \times \frac{1}{3}}{/tex} 
= 3 + 0.2 + 0.4
= 3.6 
Thus, {tex}\sqrt[3]{27}+\sqrt[3]{0.008}+\sqrt[3]{0.064}{/tex} = 3.6

Q 46

Evaluate: {[52 + (122)1/2]3}.

Solution

{[52 + (122)1/2]3}
= {[25 + (12)2x1/2]3}
= {[25 + 12]3}
= {[37]3} = 37 × 37 × 37 = 50653.

Q 47

Evaluate: {tex}\left\{\left(6^{2}+\left(8^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}\right)\right\}^{3}{/tex}

Solution

We have,{tex}\left\{\left(6^{2}+\left(8^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}\right)\right\}^{3}{/tex}
= {36 + 8)3 = (44)3 = 44 {tex}\times{/tex} 44 {tex}\times{/tex} 44 = 85184

Q 23

15% increase in the price of an article, which is ₹1620, is the increase of ₹ ________.

Solution

Let x is the price of the article
Thus, as per given question;,
1620 = x + x {tex}\times{/tex}({tex}\frac {15}{ 100}{/tex})
1620 = {tex}\frac {115x}{ 100}{/tex}
115 x = 1620 {tex}\times{/tex} 100
x - {tex}\frac {(1620 \times 100) }{ 115}{/tex}
x = 1408
Hence, increase in price = 1620 - 1408 = 212.

Q 38

By selling an article for  ₹112000, a girl gains 40%. The cost price of the article was ________.

Solution

Selling price of the article = ₹112000
Gain% = 40%
Say, x is the cost price of the article.
Since, cost price = selling price - profit % on cost price
Therefore, Selling price = cost price + profit % on cost price
Hence,
112000 = x + x {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}\frac {40}{100}{/tex})
112000 = x + ({tex}\frac 25{/tex})x
112000 = {tex}\frac {7x}5{/tex} x = {tex}\frac {(112000 \times 5)}7{/tex}
x = 80000

Q 39

Fill in the blank: The loss percent on selling 140 geometry boxes at the loss of SP of 10 geometry boxes is equal to ________.

Solution

Let the SP of 1 geometry box be ₹ 1 Then, SP of 10 geometry boxes = ₹ 10
Then according to question loss on 140 geometry boxes = ₹ 10
And SP of 140 geometry boxes = ₹140
Hence CP of 140 geometry boxes = loss + SP = ₹(10 + 140) = ₹ 150
Therefore loss% = {tex}\frac{10}{150} \times 100{/tex} 
= 6.67 %.

Solution

= {tex}\frac {2000}5{/tex} = 400%

Q 66

In a factory, women are 35% of all the workers, the rest of the workers are men. The number of men exceeds that of women by 252. Find the total number of workers in the factory.

Solution

We have, Percentage of women in factory = 35%
Percentage of men in factory = 100 - 35 = 65%
Let the total number of persons in the factory be x.
According to the question, {tex}x \times \frac{65}{100}-x \times \frac{35}{100}{/tex} = 252
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{65 x-35 x}{100}={/tex} 252
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{30 x}{100}=252{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{2520}{3}=840{/tex}
Hence, the total number of workers in the factory is 840.

Q 67

Three bags contain 64.2 kg of sugar. The second bag contains {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex} of the contents of the first and the third contains {tex}45 \frac{1}{2} \%{/tex} of what there is in the second bag. How much sugar is there in each bag?

Solution

We have given,
The total weight of sugar in three bags = 64.2 kg
Let the first bag contains x kg sugar
The second bag contains = {tex}x \times \frac{4}{5} \mathrm{kg}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{4 x}{5} \mathrm{kg}{/tex}
Third bag contains = {tex}x \times \frac{4}{5} \times \frac{91}{2} \%{/tex} 
= {tex}x \times \frac{4}{5} \times \frac{\frac{91}{2}}{100}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{91 x}{250} \mathrm{kg}{/tex}
According to the question,
 {tex}x+\frac{4 x}{5}+\frac{91 x}{250}{/tex} = 64.2
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{250 x+200 x+91 x}{250}{/tex} = 64.2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 541x = 64.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 250
541 x = 16050
{tex}x=\frac{16050}{541}{/tex} 
{tex}x=29.67 \mathrm{kg}{/tex}
So, the first bag contains the sugar = 23.73 kg
Second bag contains the sugar = 29.67{tex}\times\frac{4}{5}{/tex} 
{tex}=23.73 \mathrm{kg}{/tex}
and third bag contains the sugar = {tex}\frac{91}{250} \times 29.67{/tex}
= 10.8kg

Q 68

Find the Selling Price(SP), if Marked Price(MP) = ₹5450 and discount = 5%

Solution

Marked price = ₹5450
Discount %= 5%
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Selling price = Marked price - {tex}\frac{\text { Discount } \%}{100} \times \text { Marked price }{/tex}
= 5450 - {tex}\frac{5}{100} \times 5450{/tex}
= 5450 - 272.5 = ₹ 5177.5

Q 69

Find the Selling Price(SP) of an article, if Marked Price (MP) = ₹1300 and discount = 1.5%

Solution

Marked price (MP) = ₹1300
Discount % = 1.5%
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Selling price = Marked price - {tex}\frac{\text { Discount } \%}{100} \times \mathrm{MP}{/tex}
= {tex}1300-\frac{1.5}{100} \times 1300{/tex} = 1300 - 19.5 = ₹1280.5

Q 70

Find the Marked Price(MRP) of an article, if selling price(SP) = ₹495 and discount = 1%.

Solution

Selling price (SP) = ₹495
Discount % = 1%
Let the marked price be ₹x.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Selling price = Marked price - {tex}\frac{\text { Discount } \%}{100} \times \mathrm{MP}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 495=x-\frac{1}{100} \times x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 495=\frac{100 x-x}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 495=\frac{99 x}{100}{/tex} [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 99x = 49500
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 500
Hence, the marked price = ₹500.

Q 71

Find the Marked price(MP) of an article, if selling price(SP) = ₹ 9250 and discount = {tex}7 \frac{1}{2} \%{/tex}.

Solution

Selling price (SP) = ₹9250
Discount % = {tex}7 \frac{1}{2} \%=\frac{15}{2} \%{/tex}
Let marked price be ₹x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Selling price = Marked price - {tex}\frac{\text { Discount } \%}{100} \times \mathrm{marked\;price}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 9250=x-\frac{15}{2 \times 100} \times x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 9250=\frac{200 x-15 x}{200}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 9250=\frac{185 x}{200}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{9250 \times 200}{185}{/tex} [by cross-multiplication]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{1850000}{185}{/tex} = ₹10000
Hence, the marked price = ₹10000

Q 72

Find discount percent when the marked price (MP) of an article is ₹625 and selling price(SP) is ₹562.50.

Solution

Marked price (MP) = ₹625
Selling price (SP) = ₹562.50
{tex}\because{/tex} Discount = Marked price - Selling price = ₹625 - ₹562.5 = ₹62.5
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Discount % = {tex}\frac{\text { Discount }}{\text { Marked price }} \times 100=\frac{62.5}{625} \times 100=\frac{6250}{625}{/tex} = 10%

Q 73

Find discount percent when marked price of an article = ₹900 and selling price(SP) = ₹873.

Solution

We have,
Discount = Marked price - Selling price
= ₹ 900 - ₹ 873 
= ₹ 27
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Discount % = {tex}\frac{\text { Discount }}{\text { Marked price }} \times 100{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{27}{900} \times 100{/tex} 
= 3%

Q 74

The marked price of an article is ₹500. The shopkeeper gives a discount of 5% and still makes a profit of 25%. Find the cost price of the article.

Solution

Given, the marked price of an article = ₹500
Discount % = 5% But it makes a profit of 25%
Let the cost price of the article be ₹ x
Cost price after 5% discount = 500 - {tex}\frac{5}{100} \times 500{/tex}
= 500 - 25 = ₹475
According to the question, (100+25)% of x = 475
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{125}{100} \times x=475{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{475 \times 100}{125}{/tex}
= 3.8 {tex}\times{/tex} 100 = ₹380
Hence, the cost price of article is ₹380.

Q 75

In 2007- 08, the number of students appeared for the Class X examination were 105332 and in 2008 - 09 the number were 116054. If 88151 students pass the examination in 2007- 08 and 103804 students in 2008 - 09 then what is the increase or decrease in pass percentage in class X result?

Solution

We have, Number of students appeared in 2007-08 = 105332
Number of students appeared in 2008-09 = 116054
Number of students passed in 2007-08 = 88151
Number of students passed in 2008-09 = 103804
Passed percentage of students in 2007-08 = {tex}\frac {Number\; of\; students\; passed \;in \;2007-08} {Number \;of \;students\; appeared\; in \;2007-08} \times {100}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{88151}{105332} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{8815100}{105332}=83.68 \%{/tex}
Passed percentage of students in 2008-09
= {tex}\frac {Number\; of\; students\; passed \;in \;2008-09} {Number \;of \;students\; appeared\; in \;2008-09} \times {100}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{103804}{116054} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{10380400}{116054}=89.44 \%{/tex}
Therefore, Increase in percentage = 89.44 - 83.68 = 5.76%

Q 76

A watch worth ₹5400 is offered for sale at ₹4500. What percent discount is offered during the sale?

Solution

Marked price of a watch = ₹5400
Selling price = ₹4500
Discount = Marked price - Selling price = ₹5400 - ₹4500 = ₹900
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Discount% = {tex}\frac{\text { Discount }}{\text { Marked price }} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{900}{5400} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{900}{54}=\frac{450}{27}=\frac{150}{9}=\frac{50}{3}{/tex}%

Q 77

In the year 2001, the number of malaria patients admitted in the hospitals of a state was 4375. Every year this number decreases by 8%. Find the number of patients in 2003.

Solution

The number of malaria patients admitted in a hospital in 2001 = 4375
Rate of decrement of malaria patients = 8%
time period = 2 years [{tex}\because{/tex} 2003 - 2001 = 2 years]
Let the number of patients in 2003 be A
By using the formula, {tex}A=P\left(1-\frac{R}{100}\right)^{T}{/tex}
{tex}A=4375\left(1-\frac{8}{100}\right)^{2}{/tex}
{tex}A=4375 \times \frac{23}{25} \times \frac{23}{25}{/tex}
{tex}A=7 \times 23 \times 23{/tex} = 3703
Hence, the number of patients in 2003 will be 3703

Q 78

Jyotsana bought a product for ₹3155 including 4.5% sales tax. Find the price before tax was added.

Solution

A product bought by Jyotsana for ₹3155 including 4.5% sales tax.
Let the price of the product before sales tax be ₹ x.
S0, {tex}x+x \times \frac{4.5}{100}{/tex} = 3155
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x+\frac{x \times 45}{1000}{/tex} = 3155
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{1045 x}{1000}{/tex} = 3155 [{tex}\because{/tex} sales tax included in selling price]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{3155 \times 1000}{1045}=\frac{3155000}{1045}{/tex} = ₹ 3019.1387
= x =  ₹3019.1387
Hence, the price of the product before sales tax is ₹3019.1387

    Water used for sanitation = 40L

Q 80

In 1975, the consumption of water for human use was about 3850 cu km/yr. It increased to about 6000 cu km/yr in the year 2000. Find the per cent increase in the consumption of water from 1975 to 2000. Also, find the annual per cent increase in consumption (assuming water consumption increases uniformly).

Solution

The consumption of water for human in 1975 = 3850 cu km/yr
The consumption of water for human in 2000 = 6000 cu km/yr
Increase in consumption of water in 1975 to 2000 = 6000 - 3850 = 2150 cu km/yr
In percentage = {tex}\frac{2150}{3850} \times 100=55.84 \%{/tex}
In 25 yr total increase in water consumption = 2150 cu km/yr
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Annually, i.e. per year water consumption = {tex}\frac{2150}{25}{/tex} = 86 cu km/yr [{tex}\because{/tex}2000 -1975 = 25yr]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} In percentage = {tex}\frac{86}{3850} \times 100=\frac{8600}{3850}=2.23 \%{/tex}

Q 82

Given, principal = ₹40000, rate of interest = 8% per annum compounded annually. Find

  1. Interest if period is one year.
  2. Principal for Ilnd year.
  3. Interest for Ilnd year.
  4. Amount if period is two year.
Solution

We have given that principal (P)= ₹40000
Rate of interest (R) = 8% per annum

  1. Compound interest for one year,
    We know that, {tex}A=P\left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^{n}{/tex}
    ,= {tex}40000\left(1+\frac{8}{100}\right)^{1} [\because n=1 y r]{/tex}
    = {tex}40000 \times \frac{108}{100}{/tex}
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} Amount, A = 400 {tex}\times{/tex} 108
    = ₹43200
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} Compound interest, Cl = A - P
    = ₹43200 - ₹40000
    = ₹3200
  2. Amount of 1st year = Principal of Ilnd year
    = ₹43200
  3. Now, for Ilnd year,
    Principal = ₹43200
    Rate of interest, R= 8% per annum
    Time, n = 1 yr
    Amount for llnd year = 43200
    = {tex}\left(1+\frac{8}{100}\right)^1{/tex}
    = {tex}43200 \times \frac{108}{100}{/tex} 
    = ₹46656
    Compound interest, Cl = A - P
    = ₹46656 - ₹43200
    =  ₹3456
  4. Now, if period i.e. time (n) = 2 yr,
    Principal = ₹ 40000
    and rate (R) = 8% per annum
    {tex}\because \quad A=P\left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^{n}{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow \quad A=40000\left(1+\frac{8}{100}\right)^{2}{/tex}
    {tex}=40000 \times \frac{108}{100} \times \frac{108}{100}{/tex} 
    = ₹46656
    Therefore the total Amount, A = ₹46656

Q 88

If principal = ₹100000, rate of interest = 10% compounded half-yearly. Find

  1. Interest for 6 months.
  2. Amount after 6 months.
  3. Interest for the next 6 months.
  4. Amount after one year.
Solution

We have given that Principal (P) = ₹100000
Rate of interest (R) = 10% compounded half-yearly

  1. Interest for 6 months, We know that, Compound interest, Cl = A - P
    where, {tex}A=P\left(1+\frac{R}{200}\right)^{n}{/tex} [for 6 months]
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}100000\left(1+\frac{10}{200}\right)^1{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} n = 1 for 6 months]
    = {tex}100000 \times \frac{21}{20}{/tex} 
    = ₹105000
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} Compound interest, Cl = A - P
    = ₹105000 - ₹100000
    = ₹ 5000
  2. Amount after 6 months = ₹105000
  3. Interest for next 6 months
    Principal = Amount after 6 months
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} Principal (P) =  ₹105000
    Rate of interest (R) = 10%
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}A=P\left(1+\frac{R}{200}\right)^{n}{/tex}
    = {tex}105000\left(1+\frac{10}{200}\right)^{1}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} n = 1 for 6 months]
    = {tex}105000 \times \frac{21}{20}{/tex}
    = {tex}\frac{2205000}{20}{/tex} = ₹110250
    Therefore, Compound interest, Cl = A - P
    = ₹110250 - ₹105000
    = ₹5250
  4. Amount after one year = ₹110250
Q 89

Babita bought 160 kg of mangoes at  ₹48 per kg. She sold 70% of the mangoes at ₹70 per kg and the remaining mangoes at ₹40 per kg. Find Babita's gain or loss per cent on the whole dealing.

Solution

Babita bought 160 kg of mangoes = ₹48 per kg
So, total amount she paid = ₹48 {tex}\times{/tex} 160 = ₹7680
She sold 70% of mangoes at ₹70 per kg.
{tex}\because{/tex} Cost of 70% mangoes = {tex}160 \times \frac{70}{100} \times 70=70 \times 16 \times 7{/tex} = ₹7840
Remaining mangoes = (100 - 70)% = 30%
Cost of remaining at ₹40 per kg = {tex}160 \times \frac{30}{100} \times 40=16 \times 3 \times 40{/tex} = ₹1920
Total amount received after selling mangoes = ₹7840 + ₹1920 = ₹9760
{tex}\therefore{/tex} SP > CP so there is a gain. Gain = SP - CP = ₹9760 - ₹7680 = ₹2080
{tex}\because \quad \text { Gain } \%=\frac{\text { Gain }}{\mathrm{CP}} \times 100{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad \text { Gain } \%=\frac{2080}{7680} \times 100=27.08 \%{/tex}

Q 90

A shopkeeper was selling all his items at 25% discount. During the offseason, he offered 30% discount over and above the existing discount. If Pragya bought a skirt which was marked for ₹1200, how much did she pay for it?

Solution

Marked price of the skirt = ₹1200
During normal season discount @ 25% = {tex}\frac{25}{100} \times 1200=25 \times 12{/tex} = ₹300
Price of the skirt after discount = ₹1200 - ₹300 = ₹ 900
In the off-season, the shopkeeper also offer discount @ 30% = {tex}\frac{30}{100} \times 900{/tex} = ₹270
Price of skirt after 30% discount = ₹900 - ₹270 = ₹630
So, ₹630 paid by Pragya for the skirt.

Q 92

Find the difference between compound interest and simple interest on ₹45000 at 12% per annum for 5yr.

Solution

We have,
Principal (P) = ₹45000
Rate of interest (P) = 12% per annum
Time period (T) = 5yr
Simple interest, SI = {tex}\frac{P \times R \times T}{100}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{45000 \times 12 \times 5}{100}{/tex}
= 450 {tex}\times{/tex} 60
= ₹27000
Compound interest, Cl = A - P
where, {tex}A=P\left(1+\frac{R}{100}\right)^{T}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A = 45000{tex}\left(1+\frac{12}{100}\right)^{5}{/tex} 
= {tex}45000\left(\frac{28}{25}\right)^{5}{/tex}
= {tex}45000 \times \frac{28}{25} \times \frac{28}{25} \times \frac{28}{25} \times \frac{28}{25} \times \frac{28}{25}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{45000 \times 17210368}{9765625}{/tex} 
= ₹79200
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Compound interest, Cl = ₹79200 - ₹45000
= ₹34200
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Difference between SI and Cl = ₹ 34200 - ₹27000
= ₹7200

Q 94

= 190 {tex}\times{/tex} 20
= ₹3800
Also, on the another transaction of other cooler she has a loss of 5%.

{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 3990=\frac{19 x}{20}{/tex}
{tex}x=\frac{3990 \times 20}{19}{/tex}
{tex}x=210 \times 20{/tex} 
= ₹ 4200

Ashima sold both coolers = ₹ 3990 {tex}\times{/tex} 2
= ₹ 7980

So, Ashima has loss on overall transaction,
{tex}\because{/tex} Loss = ₹ 8000 - ₹7980
= ₹ 20

 {tex} \text { Loss } \%=\frac{1}{4} \%{/tex}  
= 0.25%

Q 100

A lady buys some pencils for ₹3 and an equal number for ₹6. She sells them for ₹7. Find her gain or loss%.

Solution

Let the Lady buys x number of pencils for ₹3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost price for one pencil, CP1 = ₹{tex}\frac{3}{x}{/tex}
Also, she buys the same number of pencils for ₹6
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost price for one pencil, CP2 = ₹{tex}\frac{6}{x}{/tex}
Now, total pencils = x + x = 2x
Selling price of 2x pencils = ₹7
selling price of 1 pencil = ₹{tex}\frac{7}{2x}{/tex}
Case I: We know that,
Gain = SP - CP
{tex}\therefore{/tex}Gain = ₹{tex}\left(\frac{7}{2 x}-\frac{3}{x}\right){/tex} = ₹{tex}\left(\frac{7-6}{2 x}\right){/tex} = ₹{tex} \frac{1}{2 x}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \text { Gain } \%=\frac{\text { gain }}{\mathrm{CP}} \times 100=\frac{\frac{1}{2 x}}{\frac{3}{x}} \times 100=\frac{100}{2 \times 3}=\frac{50}{3} \%{/tex}
Case II:
Also, we know that,
Loss = CP - SP
Loss = {tex}\frac{6}{x}-\frac{7}{2 x}=\frac{6 \times 2-7}{2 x}{/tex} =  ₹{tex} \frac{5}{2 x}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \text { Loss } \%=\frac{\text { Loss }}{\mathrm{CP}} \times 100=\frac{\frac{5}{2 x}}{\frac{6}{x}} \times 100=\frac{250}{6}=\frac{125}{3} \%{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \text { Net gain } \%=\frac{125}{3}-\frac{50}{3}=\frac{75}{3}=25 \%{/tex}

Q 101

On selling a chair of ₹736, a shopkeeper suffers a loss of 8%. At what price should he sell it, so as to gain 8%?

Solution

Let the cost price of a chair = ₹x
Selling price of a chair = ₹736
At selling a shopkeeper suffers 8% loss.
According to the question, {tex}x-x \times \frac{8}{100}=736{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{92 x}{100}=736{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{73600}{92}{/tex} = 800
To gain 8% profit the price should be = 800 + {tex}\frac{8}{100} \times 800{/tex} = 800 + 64 = ₹864

Q 102

A dining table is purchased for ₹3200 and sold at a gain of 6%. If a customer pays sales tax at the rate of 5%. How much does the customer pay in all for the table?

Solution

The cost price of the dining tables ₹3200
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The dining table sold at a gain of 6%.
So, selling price of the dining table = 3200 + {tex}\frac{6}{100} \times{/tex} 3200= 3200 + 192 = ₹3392
Also, customer pay sale tax = 5%
Selling price with sales tax = 3392 + 3392 {tex}\times \frac{5}{100}{/tex} = 3392 + 169.6 = ₹3561.60

Q 103

Achal bought a second-hand car for ₹225000 and spend ₹25000 on repairing it. If she sold it for ₹325000, what is her profit percent?

Solution

The cost price of second hand car = ₹225000
Also, Achal spends for repairing = ₹25000
So, actual cost for car = ₹225000 + ₹25000 = ₹250000
She sold the car = ₹325000
Profit = ₹ 325000 - ₹250000 = ₹75000
Profit % = {tex}\frac{\text { Profit }}{\mathrm{CP}} \times 100=\frac{75000}{250000} \times 100=\frac{7500}{250}{/tex} = 30%

Q 104

A lady bought an air-conditioner for ₹15200 and spent ₹300 and ₹500 on its transportation and repair, respectively. At what price should she sell it to make a gain of 15%?

Solution

The cost price of air conditioner = ₹15200
Also, spent amount on transportation = ₹300
Spent amount on repair = ₹500
Actual cost price of air conditioner with transportation charge and repair charges = ₹15200 + ₹300 + ₹ 500 = ₹15500 + ₹500 = ₹16000
For gain 15% she should sell it for = 16000 + 16000 {tex}\times \frac{15}{100}{/tex} = 16000 + 160 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 16000 + 2400 = ₹18400

Q 105

What price should a shopkeeper mark on article that costs him ₹600 to gain 20%, after allowing a discount of 10%?

Solution

We have given that,
The cost price of the article = ₹ 600
Gain% = 20%
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total Gain = {tex}\frac{600 \times 20}{100}{/tex} =  ₹ 120
{tex}\because{/tex} SP = Gain + CP = ₹600 + ₹120 = ₹ 720
Let marked price be ₹ x.
Now shopkeeper allows a discount of 10%
According to the question, x -10% of x = ₹720
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x-\frac{10 \times x}{100}=720{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{100 x-10 x}{100}=720{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{90 x}{100}=720{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{720 \times 100}{90}{/tex}
x =  ₹ 800
Hence, the required marked price is ₹ 800.

Q 106

Brinda purchased 18 coats at the rate of ₹1500 each and sold them at a profit of 6%. If customer is to pay sales tax at the rate of 4%, how much will one coat cost to the customer and what will be the total profit earned by Brinda after selling all coats?

Solution

We have,
The cost of each coat = ₹1500
The total number of coat = 18
The total cost of 18 coat = ₹1500 {tex}\times{/tex} 18
= ₹ 27000
If Brinda sold them at profit = 6%
The amount received by Brinda = 27000 + 27000 {tex}\times \frac{6}{100}{/tex} 
= 27000 + 270 {tex}\times{/tex} 6
= 27000 + 1620
= ₹28620
If customer pay sale tax = 4%
So, cost price with sale tax = 28620 + {tex}\frac{4}{100} \times 28620{/tex} + 1144.8
= ₹ 29764.80
Therefore, Cost price of one coat for customer = {tex}\frac{29764.8}{18}{/tex}
= ₹ 1653.60
and profit earned by Brinda = ₹ 28620 - ₹ 27000
= ₹1620

Q 107

Rahim borrowed ₹10,24,000 from a bank for one year. If the bank charges interest of 5% per annum, compounded half-yearly, what amount will he have to pay after the given time period? Also, find the interest paid by him.

Solution

It is given that borrowed amount by Rahim (P) = ₹1024000
Time period (7) = 1 year
Interest rate (R) = 5% per annum compounded half-yearly
Let amount = A
For compounded half-yearly, A = p{tex}\left(1+\frac{R}{200}\right)^{2 T}=1024000\left(1+\frac{5}{200}\right)^{2}{/tex} {tex}\left[\because \text { in half-yearly, } R=\frac{R}{2} \text { and } T=2 T\right]{/tex}
= 1024000{tex}\times \frac{41}{40} \times \frac{41}{40}=640 \times 41 \times 41{/tex} =  ₹1075840
{tex}\because{/tex} Compound Interest, Cl = A - P = ₹1075840 - ₹1024000 = ₹51840

Q 108

The following items are purchased from a showroom: T-Shirt worth  ₹1200, Jeans worth ₹1000 and 2 skirts worth ₹1350 each. What will these items cost to Shikha if the sales tax is 7%?

Solution

On the basis of given information in the question,
Cost of T-shirt = ₹1200
Cost of Jeans = ₹1000
Cost of 2 skirts = ₹1350
Total cost = ₹1200 + ₹1000 + ₹1350 = ₹3550
sale tax = 7%
So, the total amount to be paid = 3550 + {tex}\frac{7}{100} \times 3550{/tex} = 3550 + 248.5 = ₹3798.5

Q 109

The food labels given below give information about 2 types of soup: cream of tomato and sweet corn. Use these labels to answer the given questions. (All the servings are based on a 2000 calorie diet.)

Sweet Corn
Nutrition Facts
Serving Size 1 cup (240g)
About 2 serving per Container		 Cream of Tomato
Nutrition Facts
Serving Size 1 cup (240g)
About 2 serving per Container
Amount Per Serving
Calories 90   Calories from Fat 9		 Amount Per Serving
Calories 100  Calories from Fat 20
% Daily Value* % Daily Value
Total Fat 2g  2% Total Fat 2g  3%
Saturated Fat-0g  0% Saturated Fat-1.5g  6% 
Cholesterol 0mg  0% Cholesterol 10mg  3%
Sodium 540mg  22% Sodium 690mg  29%
Total Carbohydrate 17g  6% Total Carbohydrate 17g  6%
Dietary Fibre 3 gram  14% Dietary Fibre 4 gram  18%
Sugar 5g Sugar 11g
Protein 3g Protein 2g
Vitamin A 30%  Vitamin C 10% Vitamin A 20%  Vitamin A 20%
Calcium 2%  Iron 6% Calcium 0%  Iron 8%
*Percent Daily Values are
based on a 2,000 calorie diet.		 *Percent Daily Values are
based on a 2,000 calorie diet.
  1. Which can be measured more accurately: the total amount of fat in cream of tomato soup or the total amount of fat in sweet corn soup? Explain.
  2. One serving of cream of tomato soup contains 29% of the recommended daily value of sodium for a 2000 calorie diet. What is the recommended daily value of sodium in milligrams? Express the answer upto 2 decimal places.
  3. Find the increase percent of sugar consumed if the cream of tomato soup is chosen over sweet corn soup.
  4. Calculate the ratio of calories from fat in sweet corn soup to the calories from fat in cream of tomato soup.
Solution

From the information given in the table, we can have 

  1. Serving size of 1 cup of sweet corn = 240g
    Total fat = 2g on 2%
    2% of fat in sweet com of 1 cup (with 2 serving per container)
    {tex}=\frac{2}{100} \times 240{/tex}
    {tex}=\frac{48}{10}=4.8 \mathrm{g}{/tex}
    For one serving {tex}=\frac{48}{10}=4.8 \mathrm{g}{/tex}
    Serving size of 1 cup of cream of tomato =240g
    Total fat = 2g on 3%
    3% of fat in the cream tomato of 1 cup
    {tex}=\frac{3}{100} \times 240{/tex}
    {tex}=\frac{72}{10}=7.2 \mathrm{g}{/tex}
    For one serving {tex}=\frac{72}{10}=7.2 \mathrm{g}{/tex}
    Hence, 2.4g is nearest to 2g in Comparision of 3.6g to 3 g.
  2. On the basis of the given information in the question, the cream of tomato soup contains 29 % of the recommended daily value of sodium for a 2000 calories diet.
    where, 29% of 2000 calories = 690 mg.
  3. If cream of tomato soup is chosen over sweet corn soup, the increase in sugar consumed = 11g - 5g = 6g
    [{tex}\because{/tex}  sugar in sweet corn soup = 5 g and sugar in cream of tomato soup = 11g]
    Increase {tex}\%=\frac{6}{5} \times 100=\frac{600}{5}=120 \%{/tex}
  4. Fat in sweet corn soup in calories = 9
    Fat in cream tomato soup in calories = 20
    {tex}\because{/tex} Ratio = {tex}\frac{9}{20}=\frac{9}{21}=\frac{3}{7}=3: 7{/tex}
Q 110

Music CD originally priced at Rs 120 is on sale for 25% off What is the S.P.?
Sonia and Rahul have different ways of calculating the sale price for the items they bought.

As you work on the next problem, try both of these methods to see which you prefer.

Solution

It is given that Original price of music CD = ₹120
25% discount on ₹120, if sale is applicable = {tex}120 \times \frac{25}{100}=\frac{120}{4}=₹ 30{/tex}
Discount = ₹30
Selling price after discount = ₹120 - ₹30 = ₹90

Q 111

Store A and store B both charge ₹750 for a video game. This week, the video game is on sale ₹600 at store B and for 25% off at store A. At which store is the game less expensive?

Solution

The selling price of video game available in store A and store B at rate = ₹750
Store B sells video game on sale offer at = ₹600
Store A sells video game at discount = 25%
So, the price of the video game at store A = 750 - {tex}\frac{25}{100} \times{/tex} 750 = 750 - 187.5 = ₹562.5
Hence, at store A the video game is less expensive.

Q 112

At a toy shop price of all the toys is reduced to 66% of the original price.

  1. What is the sale price of a toy that originally costs ₹90?
  2. How much money would you save on a toy costing ₹90?
Solution
  1. Original cost of the toy = ₹ 90
    If price, reduce to 66% of the original price, then price became = 90-{tex}\frac{66}{100} \times 90{/tex} = 90 - 59.4 = ₹30.6
  2. We have discounted amount = 66% of 90 = {tex}\frac{66}{100} \times 90{/tex} = ₹ 59.4
Q 113

A store is having a 25% discount on sale. Sheela has a ₹50 gift voucher and wants to use it to buy a board game marked for ₹320. She is not sure how to calculate the concession she will get. The sales clerk has suggested two ways to calculate the amount payable. Method -1 Subtract ₹50 from the price and take 25% off the resulting price. Method - 2 Take 25% off the original price and then subtract ₹50.

  1. Do you think both the methods will give the same result? If not, predict which method will be beneficial for her.
  2. For each method, calculate the amount Sheela would have to pay. Show your work.
  3. Which method do you think stores actually use? Why?
Solution
  1. Marked price of board game = ₹320
    Discount in store = 25%
    Sheela have a gift voucher value = ₹50
    in method 1: cost = ₹320 - ₹50 = ₹270
    Now, 25% discount on ₹270 = 270 - {tex}\frac{25}{100} \times 270{/tex} 
    = 270 - 67.5 = ₹202.5
    In method 2: 25% off on ₹320, then subtract ₹50
    = 320 - {tex}\frac{25}{100} \times{/tex} 320 - 50 = 320 - 80 - 50 = ₹190
    Hence, method 2 will be beneficial for her.
  2. In method 1 Sheela paid = ₹202.5
    In method 2 Sheela paid = ₹190
  3. Therefore, Method 1 will be used by stores because in this method the actual discount is less. 
Q 114

Living on your own Sanjay is looking for a one-bedroom apartment on rent. At Neelgiri apartments, rent for the first two months is 20% off. The one-bedroom rate at Neelgiri is ₹6000 per month. At Savana apartments, the first month is 50% off. The one-bedroom rate at Savana apartments is ₹7000 per month. Which apartment will be cheaper for the first two months and by how much?

Solution

The one-bedroom rate at Neelgiri Apartments = ₹6000 per month
Also, 20% off for first two months,
{tex}\because{/tex} Rent for first 2 months at Neelgiri Apartments = 2 {tex}\times\left(6000-\frac{20}{100} \times 6000\right){/tex} = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 4800 = ₹ 9600
In comparison of Savana apartments, it offers 50% off for the first month, where the rent for one-bedroom is ₹ 7000 per month.
So rent for first month at Savana appartments = 7000 {tex}-\frac{50}{100} \times 7000{/tex} = 7000 - 3500 = ₹3500
Rent for two months at Savana appartment = ₹3500 + ₹7000 = ₹10500
Hence, Neelgiri appartment will be cheaper by = ₹10500 - ₹9600 = ₹900

Q 115

For an amount, explain why a 20% increase followed by a 20% decrease is less than the original amount.

Solution

Let the original amount = ₹100
20% increase in ₹100 will be = 100 + {tex}\frac{20}{100} \times 100{/tex} = 100+ 20 = ₹120
Now, 20% decrease in ₹120 = 120 - {tex}\frac{20}{100} \times 120{/tex} = 120 - 24 = ₹96
Hence, 20% increase followed by 20% decrease is less than the original amount.

Q 116

Sunscreens block harmful ultraviolet (UV) rays produced by the sun. Each sunscreen has a Sun Protection Factor (SPF) that tells you how many minutes you can stay in the sun before you receive one minute of burning UV rays. e.g. If you apply sunscreen with SPF 15, you get one minute of UV rays for every 15 minutes you stay in the sun.

  1. A sunscreen with SPF 15 allows only {tex}\frac{1}{15}{/tex} of the sun's UV rays. What 15 per cent of UV rays does the sunscreen abort?
  2. Suppose, a sunscreen allows 25% of the sun's UV rays.
    1. What fraction of UV rays does this sunscreen block? Give your answer in lowest terms.
    2. Use your answer from part (a) calculate this sunscreen's SPF. Explain how you found your answer?
  3. A label on a sunscreen with SPF 30 claims that the sunscreen blocks about 97% of harmful UV rays. Assuming the SPF factor is accurate, is this claim true. Explain.
Solution

We have, 

  1. A sunscreen with SPF 15 allows only {tex}\frac{1}{15}{/tex}of the sun’s UV rays.
    It means = 1 - {tex}\frac{1}{15}{/tex}
    = {tex}\frac{14}{15}{/tex} of the sun’s UV rays abort by sunscreen.
    In percentage = {tex}\frac{14}{15} \times 100{/tex} 
    ={tex}\frac{1400}{15}{/tex}  
    = 93.333%
    1. Sunscreen allows 25% of the sun’s UV rays.
      {tex}\therefore{/tex} It blocks UV rays = 100 - 25
      = 75%
      = {tex}\frac{75}{100}{/tex} 
      = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
    2. Sunscreen allows 25% on - of UV rays. It means that it protects = 1 - {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
      = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} of UV rays.
      Hence, it’s an SPF 4,
  3. False,
    According to the claim, for {tex}\frac{3}{100}{/tex} effect of UV rays
    1 minute = {tex}33 \frac{1}{3} \mathrm{SPF}{/tex}
    Therefore, Affect {tex}\ne{/tex} 30 SPF claim
Q 117

A real estate agent receives ₹50000 as commission, which is 4% of the selling price. At what price does the agent sell the property?

Solution

Let price of the property =₹ x
Commission received = ₹50000
Commission percentage = 4%
According to the question, {tex}x \times \frac{4}{100}=50000{/tex} = x ={tex}\frac{50000 \times 100}{4}=50000 \times 25{/tex} = ₹1250000

Q 118

With the decrease in prices of tea by 15%, Tonu, the chaiwallah, was able to buy 2 kg more of tea with the same ₹45 that he spent each month on buying tea leaves for his chai shop. What was the reduced price of tea? What was the original price of tea?

Solution

Let chaiwallah purchase y kg tea.
and price of tea per kg = ₹x
Discount 15% per kg = x - {tex}\frac{15}{100} \times x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{85 x}{100}{/tex}
Chaiwallah can buy 2 kg extra with 15% discount.
But without discount, and xy = 45 ...(i)
and {tex}\left(\frac{85 x}{100}\right)(x+2)=45{/tex} ...(ii)
After solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), 
{tex}\frac{85}{100}(45)+\frac{85 \times 2 x}{100}=45{/tex}
{tex}45\left[1-\frac{85}{100}\right]=\frac{85 \times 2 x}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{45 \times 15}{100}=\frac{85 \times 2 x}{100}{/tex}
{tex}x=\frac{45 \times 15}{2 \times 85}{/tex}
{tex}x=\frac{135}{34}{/tex} per kg
= 3.97 per kg
Therefore, original price = 3.97 per kg
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Reduced price = {tex}\frac{85}{100} \times 3.97{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{337.45}{100}{/tex}
= 3.3748
{tex} \approx {/tex} 3.38 per kg

Q 119

Below is the Report Card of Vidit Atrey. Vidit’s teacher left the last column blank. Vidit is not able to make out, in which subject he performed better and in which he needs improvement. Complete the table to help Vidit know his comparative performance.

Assessment Report for 2009-2010
Class : 9B Name : Vidit Atrey Date : 31 March 2010
Subject Internal assessment Examination Total Final%
1. English Literature 20/25 82/100 102/125  
2. English Language 22/25 91/100 113/125  
3. Hindi Literature 18/25 67/75 85/100  
4. Hindi Language 16/25 68/75 84/100  
5. Mathematics 42/50 88/100 130/150  
6. Sanskrit 14/20 75/100 99/120  
7. Physics 45/50 90/100 135/150  
8. Chemistry 41/50 82/100 123/150  
9. Biology 43/50 87/100 130/150  
10. History and Civics 19/25 68/75 87/100  
11. Geography 17/20 71.5/80 88.5/100  
Solution

On the basis of the given data in the above table, we can calculate the final percentage of each subject that can be given below:

  1. English Literature = {tex}\frac{102}{125} \times 100={/tex} {tex}\frac{102}{5} \times 4=\frac{408}{5}=81.6 \%{/tex}
  2. English Language {tex}=\frac{113}{125} \times 100=\frac{113 \times 4}{5}=\frac{452}{5}=90.4 \%{/tex}
  3. Hindi Literature {tex}=\frac{85}{100} \times 100=85 \%{/tex}
  4. Hindi Language {tex}=\frac{84}{100} \times 100=84 \%{/tex}
  5. Mathematics {tex}=\frac{130}{150} \times 100=\frac{1300}{15}=86.67 \%{/tex}
  6. Sanskrit {tex}=\frac{89}{120} \times 100=\frac{890}{12}=74.16 \%{/tex}
  7. Physics {tex}=\frac{135}{150} \times 100=\frac{1350}{15}=90 \%{/tex}
  8. Chemistry {tex}=\frac{123}{150} \times 100=\frac{1230}{15}=82 \%{/tex}
  9. Biology {tex}=\frac{130}{150} \times 100=\frac{1300}{15}=86.66 \%{/tex}
  10. History and Civics {tex}=\frac{87}{100} \times 100=87 \%{/tex}
  11. Geography {tex}=\frac{88.5}{100} \times 100=88.5 \%{/tex}
Q 120

Sita is practicing basket ball. She has managed to score 32 baskets in 35 attempts. What is her success rate in percentage?

Solution

Sita managed to score 32 baskets in 35 attempts.
Her success rate = {tex}\frac{32}{35} \times 100=\frac{3200}{35}{/tex} = 91.428% = 91.43%

Q 121

During school hours, Neha finished 73% of her homework and Minakshi completed {tex}\frac {5}{8}{/tex} of her homework. Who must finish a greater percent of homework?

Solution

Neha finished her homework in percentage = 73% 
work left for Neha= 100 - 73 = 27%
While Minakshi completed = {tex}\frac{5}{8}{/tex} of her homework
In percentage = {tex}\frac{5}{8}\times{/tex}100 = 62.5%
work left for Minakshi = 100 - 62.5 = 37.5%
Hence, Minakshi must finished a greater percentage of homework.

Q 122

Rain forests are home to 90000 of the 250000 identified plant species in the world. What per cent of the world's identified plant species are found in rain forests?

Solution

90000 of the 250000 identified plant species in the world are specified as rain forest.
percentage = {tex}\frac{90000}{250000} \times 100=\frac{900}{25}{/tex} = 36%

Q 123

Madhu's room measures 6m {tex}\times{/tex} 3m. Her carpet covers 8 m2. What per cent of the floor is covered by the carpet?

Solution

Madhu’s room measures = 6m {tex}\times{/tex} 3m
so, area of the room = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 18m2 [{tex}\because{/tex} area of rectangle = length  breadth]
also it is given that her carpet covers = 8m2
Therefore, area covered by the carpet in percentage = {tex}\frac{8}{18} \times 100=\frac{800}{18}{/tex} = 44.4%

Q 124

The human body is made up mostly of water. In fact about 67% of a person total body weight is water. If Jyoti weights 56 kg, how much of her weight is water?

Solution

Jyoti’s weight = 56 kg and 67% of a person's total body weight is water.
Water in Jyoti's body = {tex}\frac{67}{100} \times 56=\frac{3752}{100}{/tex} = 37.52 kg

Q 125

The per cent of pure gold in 14 carat gold is about 58.3%. A 14 carat gold ring weighs 7.6 grams. How many grams of pure gold are there in the ring?

Solution

The percentage of gold in 14 carat gold = 58.3
Weight of 14 carat gold ring =7.6 g
Pure gold in 14 carat gold ring of 7.6 g = 7.6 {tex}\times \frac{58.3}{100}=\frac{443.08}{100}{/tex} = 4.431 g
Hence, pure gold in 7.6 g of 14 carat gold is equal to 4.431 g.

Q 126

A student used the proportion {tex}\frac{n}{100}=\frac{5}{32}{/tex} to find 5% of 32. What did the student do wrong?

Solution

5% of 32 will be calculated as {tex}\frac{5}{100} \times 32=\frac{32}{20}{/tex} = 1.6
But the student used the proportion {tex}\frac{n}{100}=\frac{5}{32}{/tex} to find 5% of 32 is wrong.

Q 127

The table shows the cost of sunscreen of two brands with and without sales tax. Which brand has a greater sales tax rate? Give the sales tax rate of each brand?

  Cost (in ₹) Cost + Tax (in ₹)
X(100 g) 70 75
Y(100 g) 62 65
Solution

Brand X sunscreen cost = ₹70
With sales tax = ₹75
Sales tax paid = ₹75 - ₹70 = ₹5
Brand Y sunscreen cost = ₹62
With sales tax = ₹65
Sales tax = ₹65 - ₹62 = ₹3
Hence, brand X has greater sales tax rate.
Now, sales tax for brand X = {tex}\frac{5}{70} \times 100=\frac{50}{7}{/tex} = 7.1%
and sales tax for brand Y = {tex}\frac{3}{62} \times 100=\frac{300}{62}{/tex} = 4.838 = 4.84%

Q 7

Amna purchased a toy for Rs 660 including sales tax. If the rate of sales tax is 10%, then the selling price of the toy is ___.

Solution

Amna purchased a toy for Rs. 660. The rate of sales tax is 10%.
Let the sellling price of the toy be x.
x + {tex}\frac {10}{100}{/tex}x = 660
x + {tex}\frac {1}{10}{/tex}x = 660
{tex}\frac {10x + 1x}{10}{/tex}= 660
{tex}\frac {11x}{10}{/tex}= 660
11x = 6600
x = 600

Q 8

When the interest is compounded half yearly, the number of conversion periods in a year is four.

Q 9

Arnav buys a book costing ₹ 600. If the rate of sales tax is 7%, then the total amount payable by him is ₹ 642.

Q 10

After allowing a discount of 15% on the marked price of an article, it is sold for Rs 680. The marked price of the article is Rs 800.

Q 11

Overhead charges, if any, are sometimes included in the cost price.

Q 12

A number is increased by 20% and then it is decreased by 20%. Find the net increase or decrease percentage.

Solution

Let the number be 100
Increase in the number = 20% of 100 = 20
So, the increased number = 100 + 20 = 120
Decrease in the number = 20% of 120 = {tex}\frac{20}{100} \times 120{/tex} = 24
So, the new number = 120 - 24 = 96
Net decrease = 100 - 96 = 4
Hence, the net decrease percentage = {tex}\frac{4}{100} \times 100{/tex} = 4%

Q 13

Vishakha offers a discount of 20% on all the items at her shop and still makes a profit of 12%. What is the cost price of an article marked at Rs 280?

Solution

We have,
Marked Price = Rs 280
Discount = 20% of Rs 280
{tex}=\frac{20}{100} \times 280{/tex} 
= Rs 56
So, selling price = Rs (280 – 56)
= Rs 224
Now, Let the cost price be Rs 100
Profit = 12% of Rs 100
= Rs 12
So, selling price = Rs (100 + 12) = Rs 112
If the selling price is Rs 112 then cost price = Rs 100
If the selling price is Rs 224 then cost price = Rs {tex}\left(\frac{100}{112} \times 224\right){/tex}
= Rs 200.

Q 14

Find the compound interest on Rs.48,000 for one year at 8% per annum when compounded half-yearly.

Solution

It is given that Principal (P) = Rs.48,000
Rate (R) = 8% p.a.
Time (n) = 1 year
Interest is compounded half-yearly
{tex}\therefore A=P\left(1+\frac{R}{200}\right)^{2 n}{/tex}
{tex}=48,000\left(1+\frac{8}{200}\right)^{2}{/tex}
= 48,000 {tex}\times \frac{26}{25} \times \frac{26}{25}{/tex}
= 76.8 {tex}\times{/tex} 26 {tex}\times{/tex} 26
= Rs.51,916.80
Therefore, Compound Interest = A - P
= Rs.519,16.80  -  Rs.48,000
= Rs.3,916.80

Q 2

Which of the following is an identity?

Q 3

a(b + c) = ab + ac is

Q 4

(x + a) (x+ b) = x2 + (a + b) x + ________.

Q 5

An identity is true for all values of its variables.

Q 6

Simplify: -pqr (p2 + q2 + r2).

Solution

We have: - pqr (p2 + q2 + r2)
= - pqr (p2 + q2 + r2) 
= - (pqr) {tex}\times{/tex} p2 - (pqr) {tex}\times{/tex} q2 - (pqr) {tex}\times{/tex} r2
= - p3qr - pq3r - pqr3

Q 7

Simplify : (px + qy) (ax - by).

Solution

We have (px + qy) (ax - by)
(px + qy) (ax - by) = px (ax - by) + qy (ax - by)
= apx2 - pbxy + aqxy - qby2

Q 8

Find the expansion using suitable identity: (3x + 7y) (3x - 7y).

Solution

We have (3x + 7y) (3x - 7y)
(3x + 7y) (3x - 7y) = (3x)2 - (7y)2 [using identity (a + b) (a - b) = a2 - b2]
= 9x2 - 49y2

Q 9

Find the expansion using suitable identity: {tex}\left(\frac{4 x}{5}+\frac{y}{4}\right)\left(\frac{4 x}{5}+\frac{3 y}{4}\right){/tex}.

Solution

We have, {tex}\left(\frac{4 x}{5}+\frac{y}{4}\right)\left(\frac{4 x}{5}+\frac{3 y}{4}\right){/tex} 
[using the identity, (x + a) (x + b) = x2 + (a + b)x + ab]
= {tex}\left(\frac{4 x}{5}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{y}{4}+\frac{3 y}{4}\right) \frac{4 x}{5}+\frac{y}{4} \times \frac{3 y}{4}{/tex} 
= [Here, x = {tex}\frac{4 x}{5}{/tex}, a = {tex}\frac{y}{4}{/tex} and b = {tex}\frac{3 y}{4}{/tex}]
= {tex}\frac{16 x^{2}}{25}+\frac{4 y}{4} \times \frac{4 x}{5}+\frac{3 y^{2}}{16}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{16}{25} x^{2}+\frac{4 x y}{5}+\frac{3 y^{2}}{16}{/tex}

Q 10

Evaluate using suitable identities: (48)2.

Solution

We have (48)2
= (50 - 2)2 = (50)2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 50 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + (2)2 [Since (a - b)2 = a2 - 2ab + b2]
= 2500 - 200 + 4
= 2504 - 200
= 2304

Q 11

Evaluate using suitable identities: 1812 – 192.

Solution

We have 1812 - 192
1812 - 192 = (181 - 19) (181 + 19)[using a2 - b2 = (a - b) (a + b)]
= 162 {tex}\times{/tex} 200 = 32400

Q 12

Evaluate using suitable identities: 497 {tex}\times{/tex} 505.

Solution

We have 497 {tex}\times{/tex} 505 = (500 - 3) (500 + 5)
= 5002 + (-3 + 5) {tex}\times{/tex} 500 + (-3) (5) [using (x + a) (x + b) = x2 + (a + b) x + ab]
= 250000 + 1000 - 15
= 250985

Q 13

Evaluate using suitable identities: 2.07 {tex}\times{/tex} 1.93.

Solution

We have, 2.07 {tex}\times{/tex} 1.93
= (2 + 0.07) (2 - 0.07) 
= 22 - (0.07)2[using a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
= 4 - 0.0049
= 3.9951

Q 14

Verify that (3x + 5y)2 - 30xy = 9x2 + 25y2.

Solution

Taking L.H.S = (3x + 5y)2 - 30xy
= (3x)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3x {tex}\times{/tex} 5y + (5y)2 - 30xy [using identity (a + b)2 = a2 + 2ab + b2]
= 9x2 + 30xy + 25y2 - 30xy
= 9x2 + 25y2
= R.H.S
Hence, verified.

Q 15

Verify that (11pq + 4q)2 - (11pq - 4q)2 = 176pq2.

Solution

Taking L.H.S. (11pq + 4q)2 - (11pq - 4q)2
= (11pq + 4q + 11pq - 4q) {tex}\times{/tex} (11pq + 4q - 11pq + 4q)  [[using identity a2 - b2 = (a - b) (a + b)]
here a = 11pq + 4q and b = 11 pq - 4q]
= (22pq) (8q)
= 176 pq2
= R.H.S.
Hence Verified

Q 16

By using suitable identity, evaluate x2 + {tex}\frac{1}{x^2}{/tex}, if x + {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex} = 5.

Solution

Given that x + {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex} = 5
So, {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{2}{/tex} = 25
Now, {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{2}{/tex} = x2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex} + {tex}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)^{2}{/tex}
[Using identity (a + b)2 = a2 + 2ab + b2, with a = x and b = {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex}]
= x2 + 2 + {tex}\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right){/tex} 
= x2 + {tex}\left(\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right){/tex} + 2
Since {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{2}{/tex} = 25, therefore x2 + {tex}\frac{1}{x^{2}}{/tex} + 2 = 25
or {tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}{/tex} = 25 - 2 = 23

Q 17

Find the value of {tex}\frac{38^{2}-22^{2}}{16}{/tex}, using a suitable identity.

Solution

Taking 382 - 222 = (38 - 22) (38 + 22) [Since a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)]
= 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 60
 {tex}\therefore\frac{38^{2}-22^{2}}{16}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{16 \times 60}{16}{/tex} 
= 60

Q 18

Find the value of x, if 10000x = (9982)2 - (18)2.

Solution

Taking R.H.S. = (9982)2 - (18)2
= (9982 + 18) (9982 - 18) [using identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)]
= (10000) {tex}\times{/tex} (9964)
L.H.S. = (10000) {tex}\times{/tex} (x)
Comparing L.H.S. and R.H.S., we get
10000{tex}\times{/tex}(x) = 10000 {tex}\times{/tex} 9964
or x = 9964

Q 54

Add: 7a2bc, -3abc2, 3a2bc, 2abc2

Solution

We have, 7a2bc + (-3abc2) + 3a2bc + 2abc2 = 7a2bc - 3abc2 + 3a2bc + 2abc2
= (7a2bc + 3a2bc) + (-3abc2 + 2abc2) [grouping like term s]
= 10a2bc + (-abc2)
= 10a2bc - abc2

Q 55

Add: 9ax + 3by - cz, - 5by + ax + 3cz

Solution

We have, (9ax + 3by - cz) + (-5b y + ax + 3cz)
= 9ax + 3by -cz - 5by+ ax + 3cz
= (9ax + ax) + (3by - 5by) + (-cz + 3cz)
= 10ax - 2by + 2cz

Q 56

Add: xy2z2 + 3x2y 2z - 4x2yz2, - 9x2y2z + 3xy2z2 + x2yz2

Solution

We have, (xy2z2 + 3x2y2z - 4x2yz2)+ (-9x2y2z + 3xy2z2 + x2yz2)
= xy2z2 + 3x2y2z - 4x2yz2 - 9x2y2z + 3xy2z2 + x2yz2
= (xy2z2 + 3xy2z2) + (3x2y2z - 9x2y2z) + (-4x2yz2 + x2yz2) [grouping like terms]
= 4xy2z2 - 6x 2y2z - 3x 2yz2

Q 57

Add: 5x2 - 3xy + 4y2 - 9, 7y2 + 5xy - 2x2 + 13

Solution

We have, (5x2 - 3xy + 4y2 - 9) + (7y2 + 5xy - 2x2 + 13) = 5x2 - 3xy+ 4y2 - 9 + 7y2 + 5xy - 2x2 + 13
= (5x2 - 2x2) + (-3xy + 5xy) + (4y2 + 7y2) + (- 9 + 13) [grouping like terms]
= 3x2 + 2xy + 11y 2 + 4

Q 58

Add: 2p4- 3p3 + p2 - 5p + 7 and - 3p4 - 7p3 - 3p2 - p - 12.

Solution

We have, (2p4- 3p3 + p2 - 5p + 7) + (- 3p4 - 7p3 - 3p2 - p - 12)
= 2p4 - 3p3 + p2 - 5p + 7 - 3p4 -7p3 - 3p2 - p - 12
= (2p4 - 3p4) + (-3p3 - 7p3) + (p2 - 3p2) + (-5p - p) + (7 - 12) [grouping like terms]
= - p4 - 10p3 - 2p2 - 6p - 5

Q 59

Add: 3a (a - b + c) and 2b (a - b + c)

Solution

We have, 3a (a - b + c) + 2b (a - b + c)
= (3a2 - 3ab + 3ac) + (2ab - 2b 2 + 2bc)
= 3a2 - 3ab + 2ab + 3ac + 2bc - 2b2  [grouping like terms]
= 3a2 - ab + 3ac + 2bc - 2b2

Q 60

Add: 3a (2b + 5c), 3c (2a + 2b)

Solution

We have, 3a (2b + 5c) + 3c (2a + 2b)
= (6ab + 15ac )+ (6ac + 6bc)
= 6ab + 15ac+ 6ac + 6bc [grouping like terms]
= 6ab + 21ac + 6bc

Q 61

Subtract: 5a2b2c2 from -7a2b2c2

Solution

We have, 5a2b2c2 from -7a2b2c2
The required difference is given by -  7a2b2c2 - 5a2b2c2
= (- 7 - 5) a2b2c2 = - 12a2b2c2

Q 62

Subtract: 6x2 - 4xy + 5y2 from 8y2 + 6xy - 3x2

Solution

We have, 6x2 - 4xy + 5y2 from 8y2 + 6xy - 3x2
The required difference is given by (8y2 + 6xy - 3x2) - (6x2 - 4xy+ 5y2)
= 8y2 + 6xy- 3x2 - 6x2 + 4xy- 5y2
= (8y2 - 5y2) + (6xy + 4xy) - (3x2 + 5x2)= 3y2 + 10xy - 9x2

Q 63

Subtract: 2ab2c2 + 4a2b2c - 5a2bc2 from -10a2b2c + 4ab2c2 + 2a2bc2 

Solution

We have 2ab2c2 + 4a2b2c - 5a2bc2 subtract from -10a2b2c + 4ab2c2 + 2a2bc2 
The required difference is given by
(-10a2b2c + 4ab2c2 + 2a2bc2) - (2ab2c2 + 4a2b2c - 5a2bc2)
= - 10a2b 2c + 4ab2c2 + 2a2bc2 - 2ab2c2 - 4a2b2c + 5a2bc2
= (-10a2b2c - 4a2b2c) + (4ab2c2 - 2ab2c2) + (2a2bc2 + 5a2bc2) [grouping like terms]
= - 14a2b2c + 2ab2c2 + 7a2bc2

Q 64

Subtract: 3t4 - 4t3 + 2t2 - 6t + 6 from - 4t4 + 8t3 - 4t2 - 2t + 11

Solution

We have, 3t4 - 4t3 + 2t2 - 6t + 6 from -4t4 + 8t3 - 4t2 - 2t + 11
The required difference is given by
(-4t4 + 8t3 - 4t2 - 2t + 11) - (3t4 - 4t3 + 2t2 - 6t + 6)
= -4t4 + 8t3 - 4t2 - 2t + 11 - 3t4 + 4t3 - 2t2 + 6t - 6
= (-4t4 - 3t4) + (8t3 + 4t3) + (-4t2 - 2t2) + (-2t + 6t) + (11 - 6) [grouping like terms]
= -7t4 + 12t3 - 6t2 + 4t + 5

Q 65

Subtract: 2ab + 5bc - 7ac from 5ab - 2bc - 2ac + 10abc

Solution

We have, 2ab + 5bc - 7ac from 5ab - 2bc - 2ac + 10abc
The required difference is given by
(5ab - 2bc - 2ac + 10abc) - (2ab + 5bc - 7ac)
= 5ab - 2bc - 2ac + 10abc - 2ab - 5bc + 7ac
= (5ab - 2ab) + (-2bc - 5bc) + (-2ac + 7ac) + 10abc [groupting like terms]
= 3ab - 7bc + 5ac + 10abc

Q 66

Subtract: 7p(3q + 7p) from 8p (2q - 7p)

Solution

We have, 7p(3q + 7p) and 8p(2q - 7p)
The required difference : 8p(2p - 7q) - 7p(3q + 7p)
= 16p2 - 56pq - 21pq - 49p2
= (16p2 - 49p2)+ (-56pq - 21pq) [grouping like terms]
= -33p2 - 77pq

Q 67

Subtract: -3p2 + 3pq + 3px from 3p (-p - a - r)

Solution

We have, -3p2 + 3pq + 3px and 3p(-p - a - r)
The required difference is given by
3p(-p - a - r)- (-3p2 + 3pq + 3px) = -3p2 - 3ap - 3pr -(-3p2 + 3pq + 3px)
= -3p2 - 3ap - 3pr + 3p2 - 3pq - 3px = (-3p2 + 3p2) - 3ap - 3pr - 3qp - 3px 
= -3ap - 3pr - 3pq - 3px [grouping like terms]

Q 68

Multiply: -7pq2r3, -13p3q2r

Solution

We have, -7pq2r3, -13p3q2r
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (-7pq2r3) {tex}\times{/tex} (-13p3q2r) = (-7) {tex}\times{/tex} (-13) p4q4r4 = 91p4q4r4

Q 69

Multiply: 3x2y2z2, 17xyz

Solution

We have, 3x2y2z2 and 17xyz
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 3x2y2z2 {tex}\times{/tex} 17xyz = (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 17)x2y2z2 {tex}\times{/tex} xyz = 51x3y3z3

Q 70

Multiply: 15xy2, 17yz2

Solution

We have, 15xy2 and 17yz2
{tex}\therefore{/tex}  15xy2 {tex}\times{/tex} 17yz2 = (15 {tex}\times{/tex} 17)xy2 {tex}\times{/tex} yz2 = 255xy3z2

Q 71

Multiply: -5a2bc, 11ab, 13abc2

Solution

We have, -5a2bc, 11ab and 13abc2
{tex}\therefore{/tex}  -5a2bc {tex}\times{/tex} 11ab {tex}\times{/tex} 13abc2 = (-5 {tex}\times{/tex} 11 {tex}\times{/tex} 13) a2bc {tex}\times{/tex} ab {tex}\times{/tex} abc2 = -715a4b3c3

Q 72

Multiply: -3x2y, (5y - xy)

Solution

We have, -3x2y and (5y - xy)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} -3x2y {tex}\times{/tex} (5y - xy) = -3x2y {tex}\times{/tex} 5y + 3x2y {tex}\times{/tex} xy = -15x2y2 + 3x3y2

Q 73

Multiply: abc, (bc + ca)

Solution

We have, abc and (bc + ca)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} abc {tex}\times{/tex} (bc + ca)= abc {tex}\times{/tex} bc + abc {tex}\times{/tex} ca = ab2c2 + a2bc2

Q 74

Multiply: 7pqr, (p - q + r)

Solution

We have, 7pqr and (p - q + r)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 7pqr {tex}\times{/tex} (p - q + r) = 7pqr {tex}\times{/tex} p -7pqr {tex}\times{/tex} q + 7pqr {tex}\times{/tex} r = 7p2qr - 7pq2r + 7pqr2

Q 75

Multiply: x2y2z2 and (xy - yz + zx).

Solution

We have, x2y2z2 and (xy - yz + zx)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x2y2z2 {tex}\times{/tex} (xy - yz + zx) = x2y2z2 {tex}\times{/tex} xy - x2y2z2 {tex}\times{/tex} yz + x2y2z2 {tex}\times{/tex} zx
= x3y3z2 - x2y3z3 + x3y2z3

Q 76

Multiply: (p + 6), (q - 7)

Solution

We have, (p + 6), (q - 7)
{tex}\therefore{/tex}  (p + 6) {tex}\times{/tex} (q - 7) = p(q - 7) + 6(q - 7) = pq -7p + 6q - 42

Q 77

Multiply: 6mn, 0mn

Solution

We have, 6mn and 0mn
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 6mn {tex}\times{/tex} 0mn = (6 {tex}\times{/tex} 0) mn {tex}\times{/tex} mn = 0 {tex}\times{/tex} m2n2 = 0

Q 78

Multiply: a, a5, a6

Solution

We have, a, a5, a6
{tex}\therefore{/tex} a {tex}\times{/tex} a5 {tex}\times{/tex} a6 = a1 + 5 + 6 = a12

Q 79

Multiply: -7st, -1, -13st2

Solution

We have, -7st, -1 and -13st2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} -7st {tex}\times{/tex} (-1) {tex}\times{/tex} (-13st2) = [-7 {tex}\times{/tex} (-1) {tex}\times{/tex} (-13)]st {tex}\times{/tex} (st2) = -91s2t3

Q 80

Multiply: b3, 3b2 and 7ab5

Solution

We have, b3, 3b2 and 7ab5
{tex}\therefore{/tex} b3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3b2 {tex}\times{/tex} 7ab5 = (1 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 7)b3 {tex}\times{/tex} b2 {tex}\times{/tex} ab5 = 21ab10

Q 81

Multiply: {tex}\frac{-100}{9} rs{/tex}, {tex}\frac{3}{4} r^{3} s^{2}{/tex}

Solution

We have, {tex}\frac{-100}{9} rs{/tex} and {tex}\frac{3}{4} r^{3} s^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex}  {tex}\frac{-100}{9} r s \times \frac{3}{4} r^{3} s^{2}=\left(\frac{-100}{9} \times \frac{3}{4}\right) r s \times r^{3} s^{2}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-25}{3} \times r^{4} s^{3}{/tex}

Q 82

Multiply: (a2 - b2), (a2 + b2)

Solution

We have, (a2 - b2) and (a2 + b2)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (a2 - b2) (a2 + b2) = a2(a2 + b2) - b2(a2 + b2)= a4 + a2b2 - b2a2 - b4 = a4 - b4

Q 83

Multiply: (ab + c), (ab + c).

Solution

We have, (ab + c) and (ab + c)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (ab + c)(ab + c) = ab(ab + c) + c(ab + c)

Q 85

Multiply: {tex}\left(\frac{3}{4} x-\frac{4}{3} y\right){/tex}, {tex}\left(\frac{2}{3} x+\frac{3}{2} y\right){/tex}

Solution

We have,  {tex}\left(\frac{3}{4} x-\frac{4}{3} y\right){/tex} and {tex}\left(\frac{2}{3} x+\frac{3}{2} y\right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\left(\frac{3}{4} x-\frac{4}{3} y\right){/tex}{tex}\left(\frac{2}{3} x+\frac{3}{2} y\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{4} x\left(\frac{2}{3} x+\frac{3}{2} y\right)-\frac{4}{3} y\left(\frac{2}{3} x+\frac{3}{2} y\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3}{4} \times \frac{2}{3} x^{2}+\frac{3}{4} \times \frac{3}{2} x y-\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{2}{3} y x-\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{3}{2} y^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} x^{2}+\frac{9}{8} x y-\frac{8}{9} x y-2 y^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} x^{2}+\left(\frac{9}{8}-\frac{8}{9}\right) x y-2 y^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} x^{2}+\left(\frac{81-64}{72}\right) x y-2 y^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} x^{2}+\frac{17}{72} x y-2 y^{2}{/tex}

Q 86

= {tex}\frac{3}{2} p^{2} \times 2 p^{2}-\frac{9}{2} p^{2} q^{2}+\frac{4}{3} q^{2} p^{2}-2 q^{4}{/tex}
= {tex}3 p^{4}+\left(\frac{4}{3}-\frac{9}{2}\right) p^{2} q^{2}-2 q^{4}{/tex}
= {tex}3 p^{4}+\left(\frac{8-27}{6}\right) p^{2} q^{2}-2 q^{4}{/tex}
= {tex}3 p^{4}-\frac{19}{6} p^{2} q^{2}-2 q^{4}{/tex}
