x3y2 + x2y3 - xy4 + xy = xy(x2y + xy2 - y3 + 1)

Q 44

Factorise the expression: 4xy2 - 10x2y + 16x2y2 + 2xy.

Solution

We have, 
4xy2 - 10x2y + 16x2y2 + 2xy 
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2xy2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} x2y + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 {tex}\times{/tex} x2y2 + 2xy
 = 2xy (2y - 5x + 8xy + 1)

Q 45

Factorise the expression: 2a3 - 3a2b + 5ab2 - ab.

Solution

We have, 2a3 - 3a2b + 5ab2 - ab
2a3 - 3a2b + 5ab2 - ab = a(2a2 - 3ab + 5b2 - b)

Q 46

Factorise the expression: 63p2q2r2s - 9pq2r2s2 + 15p2qr2s2 - 60p2q2rs2.

Solution

We have,
63p2q2r2s - 9pq2r2s2 + 15p2qr2s2 - 60p2q2rs2 
= 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 21p2q2r2s - 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3pq2r2s2 + 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5p2qr2s2 - 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 20p2q2rs2
 = 3pqrs (21pqr - 3qrs + 5prs - 20pqs)

Q 47

Factorise the expression: 24x2yz3 - 6xy3z2 + 15x2y2z - 5xyz

Solution

We have, 24x2yz3 - 6xy3z2 + 15x2y2z - 5xyz ,it is clear that xyz is common in all terms
           so,  24x2yz3 - 6xy3z2 + 15x2y2z - 5xyz = xyz (24xz2 - 6y2z + 15xy - 5)

Q 48

Factorise the expression: a3 + a2 + a + 1.

Solution

It is given that a3 + a2 + a + 1
a3 + a2 + a + 1 = a2 (a + 1) +1 (a + 1)
= (a + 1) (a2 + 1)

Q 49

Factorise the expression: lx + my + mx + ly.

Solution

We have, lx + my + mx + ly
lx + my + mx + ly = lx + mx + my+ ly
= x(l + m) + y(m + l) 
= (l + m)(x + y)

Q 50

Factorise the expression: a3x – x4 + a2x2 – ax3

Solution

We have, = a3x - x4 + a2x2 - ax3
= x(a3 - x3 + a2x - ax2) = x (a3 + a2x - x3 - ax2)
= x [a2(a + x ) - x2(x + a)]
= x [(x + a) (a2 - x 2)]

= x(a2 - x2) (a + x)

= x(a - x)(a + x)(a + x)

Q 51

Factorise the expression: 2x2 - 2y + 4xy - x.

Solution

We have, 2x2 - 2y + 4xy - x
2x2 - 2y + 4xy - x = 2x2 - x - 2y + 4xy
= x(2x - 1) - 2y (1 - 2x)
= x (2x - 1) + 2y (2x - 1)
= (2x - 1) (x + 2y)

Q 52

Factorise the expression: y2 + 8zx - 2xy - 4yz.

Solution

We have, y2 + 8zx - 2xy - 4yz
y2 + 8zx - 2xy - 4yz
= y2 - 2xy + 8zx - 4yz
= y(y - 2x) - 4z(y - 2x)
= (y - 2x) (y - 4z)

Q 53

Factorise the expression: ax2y – bxyz – ax2z + bxy2

Solution

We have, ax2y - bxyz - ax2z + bxy2  it is clear that x is common in all terms
ax2y - bxyz - ax2z + bxy2 = x(axy - byz - axz + by2)
 = x(axy - axz - byz + by2)
= x[ax(y - z) + by(-z + y)]
= x(ax + by)(y - z)

Q 54

Factorise the given expression: a2b + a2c + ab + ac + b2c + c2b

Solution

We have, a2b + a2c + ab + ac + b2c + c2b, it can be factorise as
a2b + a2c + ab + ac + b2c + c2b = (a2b + ab + b2c) + (a2c + ac + c2b)
= b(a2 + a + bc) + c (a2 + a + bc)
= (a2 + a + bc) (b + c)

Q 55

Factorise the expression: 2ax2 + 4axy + 3bx2 + 2ay2 + 6bxy + 3by2

Solution

We have, 2ax2 + 4axy + 3bx2 + 2ay2 + 6bxy + 3by2 , it can be factorise as,
= (2ax2 + 2ay2 + 4axy) + (3bx2 + 3by2 + 6bxy)
= 2a(x2 + y2 + 2xy) + 3b(x2 + y2 + 2xy)
= (2a + 3b)(x2 + y2 + 2xy)
= (2a + 3b)(x + y)2
= (2a + 3b)(x + y)(x + y)

Q 56

Factorise x2 + 6x + 9, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2

Solution

We have, x2 + 6x + 9 = (x)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} x + (3)2
= (x + 3)2  [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2]
= (x + 3) (x + 3)

Q 57

Factorise x2 + 12x + 36, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2

Solution

We have, x2 + 12x + 36
= (x)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex}6x + (6)2 [using identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2]
= (x + 6)2
= (x + 6)(x + 6)

Q 58

Factorise x2 + 14x + 49, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2

Solution

We have, x2 + 14x + 49 
x2 + 14x + 49 = (x)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} x + (7)2 
= (x + 7)2  [using (a + b)2 = a2 + 2ab + b2 ]
= (x + 7)(x + 7)

Q 59

Factorise x2 + 2x + 1, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2.

Solution

We have, x2 + 2x + 1
x2 + 2x + 1 = x2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 {tex}\times{/tex} x + 12
 = (x + 1)2       [by using the identity (a + b)2 = a2 + 2ab + b2]
  = (x + 1) (x + 1)

Q 60

Factorise 4x2 + 4x + 1, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2.

Solution

We have, 4x2 + 4x + 1
4x2 + 4x + 1 = (2x)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2x {tex}\times{/tex} 1 + 12 
= (2x + 1)2     [{tex}\because{/tex}a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2 ]
= (2x + 1)(2x + 1)

Q 61

Factorise a2x2 + 2ax + 1, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2.

Solution

We have, a2x2 + 2ax + 1
a2x2 + 2ax + 1 = (ax)2 + 2{tex}\times{/tex}ax{tex}\times{/tex}1 + (1)2 
 = (ax +1)2  [{tex}\because{/tex}a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2 ]
 = (ax + 1) (ax + 1)

Q 62

Factorise a2x2 + 2abx + b2, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2

Solution

We have, a2x2 + 2abx + b2
= (ax)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} ax {tex}\times{/tex} b + b2
= (ax + b)2  [{tex}\because{/tex}a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2 ]
= (ax + b) (ax + b)

Q 63

Factorise a2x2 + 2abxy + b2y2, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2.

Solution

We have, a2x2 + 2abxy + b2y2
= (ax)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} ax {tex}\times{/tex} by + (by)2 
= (ax + by)2[using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2]
= (ax + by) (ax + by)

Q 64

Factorise 4x2 + 12x + 9, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2.

Solution

We have, 4x2 + 12x + 9
= (2x)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2x {tex}\times{/tex} 3 + (3)2  
= (2x + 3)2   [{tex}\because{/tex}a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2]
= (2x + 3) (2x + 3)

Q 65

Factorise 16x2 + 40x + 25, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2

Solution

We have, 16x2 + 40x + 25
16x2 + 40x + 25 = (4x)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 4x {tex}\times{/tex} 5 + (5)2
 = (4x + 5)2     [{tex}\because{/tex}a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2 ]
= (4x + 5 )(4x + 5)

Q 66

Factorise 9x2 + 24x + 16, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2

Solution

We have, 9x2 + 24x + 16
= (3x)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3x {tex}\times{/tex} 4 + (4)2
= (3x + 4)2  [{tex}\because {/tex}a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2]
= (3x + 4)(3x + 4)

Q 67

Factorise 9x2 + 30x + 25, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2

Solution

We have, 9x2 + 30x + 25
9x2 + 30x + 25 = (3x)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3x {tex}\times{/tex} 5 + (5)2   
= (3x + 5)2  [{tex}\because {/tex}a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2 ]
= (3x + 5) (3x + 5)

Q 68

Factorise 2x3 + 24x2 + 72x, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2

Solution

We have, 2x2 + 24x + 72x
= 2x (x2 + 12x + 36)
= 2x [ (x)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} x + (6)2 ]       [{tex}\because {/tex}a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2 ]
= 2x (x + 6)2
= 2x (x + 6) (x + 6)

Q 69

Factorise a2x3 + 2abx2 + b2x, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2

Solution

We have, a2x3 + 2abx2 + b2x
= x(a2x2 + 2abx + b2)
= x[(ax)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} ax {tex}\times{/tex} b + (b)2]
= x(ax + b)2           [{tex}\because {/tex} (a + b)2 = a2 + 2ab + b2]
= x(ax + b) (ax + b)

Q 70

Factorise 4x4 + 12x3 + 9x2, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2

Solution

We have, 4x4 + 12x3 + 9x2
= x2 (4x2 + 12x+ 9)
= x2[(2x)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2x {tex}\times{/tex} 3 + (3)2]   [{tex}\because {/tex}a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2
= x2(2x + 3)2
= x2(2x + 3) (2x + 3)

Q 71

Factorise {tex}\frac{x^2}{4}{/tex} + 2x + 4, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2.

Solution

We have, {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{4}+2 x+4{/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)^{2}+2 \times \frac{x}{2} \times 2+2^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{x}{2}+2\right)^{2}{/tex}[using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2]
= {tex}\left(\frac{x}{2}+2\right)\left(\frac{x}{2}+2\right){/tex}

Q 72

Factorise 9x2 + 2xy + {tex}\frac{y^2}{9}{/tex}, using the identity a2 + 2ab + b2 = (a + b)2

Solution

We have, {tex}9 x^{2}+2 x y+\frac{y^{2}}{9}{/tex}
= {tex}(3 x)^{2}+2 \cdot 3 x \cdot \frac{y}{3}+\left(\frac{y}{3}\right)^{2}=\left(3 x+\frac{y}{3}\right)^{2}{/tex}  [{tex}\because {/tex} (a + b)2 = a2 + 2ab + b2]
{tex}=\left(3 x+\frac{y}{3}\right)\left(3 x+\frac{y}{3}\right){/tex}

Q 73

Factorise x2 - 8x + 16, using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2.

Solution

We have, x2 - 8x + 16
x2 - 8x + 16 = x2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} 4 + 42
= (x - 4)2 [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2]
= (x - 4)(x - 4)

Q 74

Factorise x2 - 10x + 25, using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2.

Solution

We have,
x2 - 10x + 25 = x2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} 5 + 52
= (x - 5)2   [using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2]
 = (x - 5) (x - 5)

Q 75

Factorise y2 - 14y + 49, using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2.

Solution

We have, y2 - 14y + 49
= y2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} y {tex}\times{/tex} 7 + (7)2
= (y - 7)2 [using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2]
= (y - 7) (y - 7)

Q 76

Factorise p2 - 2p + 1, using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2.

Solution

We have, p2 - 2p + 1
= p2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} p {tex}\times{/tex} 1 + (1)2
= (p - 1)2  [using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2] 
= (p - 1)(p - 1)

Q 77

Factorise 4a2 - 4ab + b2, using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2.

Solution

We have,
4a2 - 4ab + b2 = (2a)2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2a {tex}\times{/tex} b + (b)2
= (2a - b)2 [using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2]
= (2a - b)(2a - b)

Q 78

Factorise p2y2 - 2py + 1, using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2.

Solution

We have, p2y2 - 2py + 1
= (py)2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} py {tex}\times{/tex} 1 + (1)2
= (py - 1)2 [using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2
= (py - 1) (py - 1)

Q 79

Factorise a2y2 - 2aby + b2, using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2

Solution

We have, a2y2 - 2aby + b2
= (ay)2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} ay {tex}\times{/tex} b + (b)2   [{tex}\because{/tex}a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2 ]
= (ay - b)2
= (ay - b) (ay - b)

Q 80

Factorise 9x2 - 12x + 4, using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2

Solution

We have, 9x2 - 12x + 4
= (3x)2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3x {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + (2)2 
= (3x - 2)2    [{tex}\because {/tex}a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2 ]
= (3x - 2) (3x - 2)

Q 81

Factorise: 4y2 – 12y + 9

Solution

Using identity, a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2
4y2 - 12y + 9
= (2y)2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} (2y) + (3)2
= (2y – 3)2
= (2y – 3)(2y – 3)
This is the required factorisation.

Q 82

Factorise {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{4}{/tex} - 2x + 4, using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2.

Solution

We have, {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{4}-2 x+4{/tex}
= {tex}\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)^{2}-2 \times \frac{x}{2} \times 2+(2)^{2}{/tex}  [{tex}\because{/tex}a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2
= {tex}\left(\frac{x}{2}-2\right)^{2}=\left(\frac{x}{2}-2\right)\left(\frac{x}{2}-2\right){/tex}

Q 83

Factorise a2y3 - 2aby2 + b2y, using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2.

Solution

We have, a2y3 - 2aby2 + b2y
= y(a2y2 - 2aby + b2)
= y[(ay)2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex}ay{tex}\times{/tex} b + (b)2]   [{tex}\because {/tex}a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2]
= y(ay - b)2
= y(ay - b)(ay - b)

Q 84

Factorise 9y2 - 4xy + {tex}\frac{4x^2}{9}{/tex}, using the identity a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2.

Solution

We have, {tex}9 y^{2}-4 x y+\frac{4 x^{2}}{9}{/tex}
= {tex}(3 y)^{2}-2 \cdot 3 y \cdot \frac{2}{3} x+\left(\frac{2}{3} x\right)^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(3 y-\frac{2}{3} x\right)^{2}{/tex}    [{tex}\because{/tex}a2 - 2ab + b2 = (a - b)2 ]

= {tex}\left(3 y-\frac{2 x}{3}\right)\left(3 y-\frac{2 x}{3}\right){/tex}

Q 85

Factorise x2 + 15x + 26

Solution

We have, x2 + 15x + 26
x2 + 15x + 26 = x2 + 2x + 13x + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 13
= x (x + 2) + 13 (x + 2)
  = (x + 2)(x + 13)

Q 86

Factorise x2 + 9x + 20.

Solution

We have, x2 + 9x + 20
x2 + 9x + 20 = x2 + 5x + 4x + 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
= x (x + 5) + 4 (x + 5 ) 
= (x + 5) (x + 4)

Q 87

Factorise y2 + 18x + 65.

Solution

We have, y2 + 18y + 65
= y2 + 13y + 5y + 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 13 [using middle term splitting]
= y(y + 13) + 5(y + 13)
= (y + 13)(y + 5)

Q 88

Factorise p2 + 14p + 13.

Solution

We have, p2 + 14p + 13
= p2 + 13p + p + 13 {tex}\times{/tex} 1[using middle term splitting]
= p(p + 13) + 1(p + 13)
= (p + 13)(p + 1)

Q 89

Factorise y2 + 4y - 21.

Solution

We have, y2 + 4y - 21
 y2 + 4y - 21 = y2 + (7 - 3)y - 2
 = y2 + 7y - 3y - 21
= y(y + 7) - 3(y + 7)
 = (y + 7) (y - 3)

Q 90

Factorise y2 - 2y - 15.

Solution

We have, 
y2 - 2y - 15 = y2 + (3 - 5)y - 15
 = y2 + 3y - 5y - 15
= y(y + 3) - 5 (y + 3)
 = (y + 3) (y - 5)

Q 91

Factorise 18 + 11x + x2.

Solution

We have, 18 + 11x + x2 , At first we will write it in standard form as
x2 + 11x + 18 = x2 + (9 + 2)x + 18
 = x2 + 9x + 2x + 18
= x(x + 9) + 2(x + 9)
= (x + 2)(x + 9)

Q 92

Factorise x2 - 10x + 21.

Solution

We have, x2 - 10x + 21
x2 - 10x + 21 = x2 - (7 + 3)x + 21
= x2 - 7x - 3x + 21
 = x(x - 7) - 3(x - 7)
 = (x - 7) (x - 3)

Q 93

Factorise x2 - 17x + 60.

Solution

We have, x2 - 17x + 60
= x2 -(12 + 5)x + 60[using middle term splitting]
= x2 - 12x - 5x + 60
= x(x - 12) - 5(x - 12)
= (x - 12)(x - 5)

Q 94

Factorise x2 + 4x - 77

Solution

We have, x2 + 4x - 77
x2 + 4x - 77 = x2 + (11 - 7)x - 77
  = x2 + 11x - 7x - 77
= x (x + 11) - 7 (x + 11)
  = (x + 11) (x - 7)

Q 95

Factorise: y2 + 7y + 12

Solution

We have, y2 + 7y + 12
y2 + 7y + 12 = y2 + (4 + 3)y + 12
 = y2 + 4y + 3y + 12
 = y(y + 4) + 3(y + 4)
= (y + 4)(y + 3)

Q 96

Factorise p2 - 13p - 30

Solution

We have, p2 - 13p - 30
p2 - 13p - 30 = p2 - (15 - 2)p - 30 
 = p2 - 15p + 2p - 30
= p(p - 15) + 2(p - 15)
= (p - 15) (p + 2)

Q 97

Factorise p2 - 16p - 80

Solution

We have, p2 - 16p - 8
p2 - 16p - 8 = p2 - (20 - 4)p - 80 
= p2 - 20p + 4p - 80
 = p(p - 20) + 4(p - 20)
= (p - 20) (p + 4)

Q 98

Factorise x2 - 9 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, x2 - 9
x2 - 9 = x2 - (3)2
= (x - 3)(x + 3) [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 99

Factorise 4x2 - 25y2 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, 4x2 - 25y2
4x2 - 25y2 = (2x)2 - (5y)2
 = (2x - 5y)(2x + 5y)  [{tex}\because{/tex} a2- b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 100

Factorise 4x2 - 49y2 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, 4x2 - 49y2
 4x2 - 49y2 = (2x)2 - (7y)2 
= (2x - 7)(2x + 7)   [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 101

Factorise 3a2b3 - 27a4b using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have,
3a2b3 - 27a4b
= 3a2b(b2 - 9a2)
= 3a2b[b2 - (3a)2]
= 3a2b(b + 3a)(b - 3a)[{tex}\because{/tex}a2- b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]

Q 102

Factorise 28ay2 - 175ax2 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)

Solution

We have, 28ay2 - 175ax2
28ay2 - 175ax2 

= 7a(4y2 - 25x2)

= 7a [(2y)2 - (5x)2]
= 7a(2y - 5x) (2y + 5x)    [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 103

Factorise 9x2 - 1 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, 9x2 - 1
9x2 - 1 = (3x)2 - (1)2
= (3x - 1)(3x + 1)[{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 104

Factorise 25ax2 - 25a using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, 25ax2 - 25a
25ax2 - 25a
= 25a(x2 - 12)
= 25a(x - 1)(x + 1) [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 105

Factorise {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{9}-\frac{y^{2}}{25}{/tex} using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{9}-\frac{y^{2}}{25}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x^{2}}{9}-\frac{y^{2}}{25}{/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{y}{5}\right)^{2} {/tex}
={tex}\left(\frac{x}{3}-\frac{y}{5}\right)\left(\frac{x}{3}+\frac{y}{5}\right){/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 106

Factorise {tex}\frac{2 p^{2}}{25}-32 q^{2}{/tex} using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, {tex}\frac{2 p^{2}}{25}-32 q^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{2 p^{2}}{25}-32 q^{2}{/tex} = {tex}2\left(\frac{p^{2}}{25}-16 q^{2}\right){/tex}
= {tex}2\left[\left(\frac{p}{5}\right)^{2}-(4 q)^{2}\right]{/tex}

={tex}2\left(\frac{p}{5}+4 q\right)\left(\frac{p}{5}-4 q\right){/tex} [using identity a2 - b2= (a + b) (a - b)]

Q 107

Factorise 49x2 - 36y2 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, 49x2 - 36y2
49x2 - 36y2 = (7x)2 - (6y)2
= (7x - 6y)(7x + 6y)[{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 108

Factorise y3 - {tex}\frac{y}{9}{/tex} using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have,
{tex}y^3 - \frac{y}{9}{/tex} 
= {tex}y\left(y^{2}-\frac{1}{9}\right){/tex}
={tex}y\left[y^{2}-\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^{2}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}y\left(y+\frac{1}{3}\right)\left(y-\frac{1}{3}\right){/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 109

Factorise {tex}\frac{x^2}{25}{/tex} - 625, using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{25}-625{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x^{2}}{25}-625{/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{x}{5}\right)^{2}-(25)^{2}{/tex} 
={tex}\left(\frac{x}{5}-25\right)\left(\frac{x}{5}+25\right){/tex}[using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]

Q 110

Factorise {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{8}-\frac{y^{2}}{18}{/tex} using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{8}-\frac{y^{2}}{18}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x^{2}}{8}-\frac{y^{2}}{18}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{x^{2}}{4}-\frac{y^{2}}{9}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\left(\frac{x}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{y}{3}\right)^{2}\right]{/tex}
={tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{x}{2}+\frac{y}{3}\right)\left(\frac{x}{2}-\frac{y}{3}\right){/tex} [using the identity a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 111

Factorise {tex}\frac{4 x^{2}}{9}-\frac{9 y^{2}}{16}{/tex} using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, {tex}\frac{4x^{2}}{9}-\frac{9y^{2}}{16}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{4x^{2}}{9}-\frac{9y^{2}}{16}{/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{2 x}{3}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{3 y}{4}\right)^{2}{/tex}
={tex}\left(\frac{2 x}{3}+\frac{3 y}{4}\right)\left(\frac{2 x}{3}-\frac{3 y}{4}\right){/tex}[using the identity a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 112

Factorise {tex}\frac{x^{3} y}{9}-\frac{x y^{3}}{16}{/tex} using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have,
{tex}\frac{x^{3} y}{9}-\frac{x y^{3}}{16}{/tex} = {tex}x y\left(\frac{x^{2}}{9}-\frac{y^{2}}{16}\right){/tex}
= {tex}x y\left[\left(\frac{x}{3}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{y}{4}\right)^{2}\right]{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)]
= {tex}x y\left(\frac{x}{3}+\frac{y}{4}\right)\left(\frac{x}{3}-\frac{y}{4}\right){/tex}

Q 113

Factorise 1331x3y - 11y3x using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, 1331x3y - 11y3x
1331x3y - 11y3x = (11)3x3y - 11y3x 
= 11xy(112x2 - y2)
= 11xy[(11x)2 - y2]
= 11xy(11x + y)(11x - y)  [using a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]

Q 114

Factorise {tex}\frac{1}{36} a^{2} b^{2}-\frac{16}{49} b^{2} c^{2}{/tex} using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, {tex}\frac{1}{36} a^{2} b^{2}-\frac{16}{49} b^{2} c^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}b^{2}(\frac{1}{36} a^{2} -\frac{16}{49} c^{2}){/tex} 
= {tex}b^{2}\left(\frac{a }{6}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{4 c}{7}\right)^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}b^{2}\left(\frac{a}{6}+\frac{4 c}{7}\right)\left(\frac{a }{6}-\frac{4 c}{7}\right){/tex}[using identity a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)] 

Q 115

Factorise a4 - (a - b)4 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)

Solution

We have, a4 - (a - b)4 
a4 - (a - b)4 = (a2)2 - [(a - b)2]2        [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)]
= [a2 + (a - b)2] [a2 - (a - b)2]
= [a2 + a2 + b2 - 2ab] [a2 - (a2 + b2 - 2ab)]
= [2a2 + b2 - 2ab] [-b2 + 2ab]
 = (2a2 + b2 - 2ab) (2ab - b2)

Q 116

Factorise x4 - 1 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, x4 - 1
x4 - 1 = (x2)2 - 12
= (x2 + 1)(x2 - 1)[{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]
= (x2 + 1)(x + 1) (x - 1)[{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 117

Factorise y4 - 625 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, y4 - 625
y4 - 625 = (y2)2 - (25)2
= (y2 + 25) (y2 - 25)[{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]
= (y2 + 25) (y2 - 52)
= (y2 + 25)(y + 5)( y - 5)[{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 118

Factorise p5 - 16p using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)

Solution

We have, p5 - 16p

p5 - 16p = p(p4 - 16)

= p[(p2)2 - (4)2]

= p(p2 + 4)(p2 - 4)     [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]
= p(p2 + 4) (p2 - (2)2)
= p(p2 + 4) (p + 2) (p - 2)     [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 119

Factorise 16x4 - 81 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)

Solution

We have, 16x4 - 81 = (4x2)2 - (9)2 = (4x2 + 9) (4x2 - 9) 
= (4x2 + 9) [(2x)2 - (3)2]        [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
= (4x2 + 9)(2x + 3)(2x - 3)

Q 120

Factorise x4 - y4 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, x4 - y4 = (x2)2 - (y2)2 
= (x2 + y2) ( x2 - y2)      [{tex}\because{/tex}a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]
= (x2 + y2)(x + y)(x - y)   [{tex}\because{/tex}a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 121

Factorise y4 - 81 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, y4 - 81
= (y2)2 - (9)2
= (y2 + 9) (y2 - 9)[using a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
= (y2 + 9)[(y)2 - (3)2]
= (y2 + 9)(y + 3)(y - 3)[using a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]

Q 122

Factorise 16x4 - 625y4 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, 16x4 - 625y4 = (4x2)2 - (25y2)2
= (4x2 + 25y2) (4x2 - 25y2)  using identity [a2- b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
= (4x2 + 25y2) [(2x)2 - (5y)2]  again using identity [a2- b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
= (4x2 + 25y2) (2x + 5y) (2x - 5y)

Q 123

Factorise (a - b)2 - (b - c)2 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, (a - b)2 - (b - c)2
(a - b)2 - (b - c)2 = (a - b + b - c)(a - b - b + c)[using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
= (a - c)(a - 2b + c)

Q 124

Factorise (x + y)4 - (x - y)4 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, (x + y)4 - (x - y)4 = [(x + y)2]2 - [(x - y)2]2
= [(x + y)2 + (x - y)2][(x + y)2 - (x - y)2]  using the identity [a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)]
= (x2 + y2 + 2xy + x2 + y2 - 2xy) (x + y + x - y) (x + y - x + y)  again using the identity [a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)]
= (2x2 + 2y2)(2x)(2y)
= 2(x2 + y2)(2x)(2y)

= 8xy(x2 + y2)

Q 125

Factorise x4 - y4 + x2 - y2 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

x4 - y4 + x2 - y2 = (x2)2 - (y2)2 + (x2 - y2)  [using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)]
= (x2 + y2)(x2 - y2) + (x2 - y2)    [again using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)]
= (x2 - y2)(x2 + y2 + 1)
= (x + y) (x - y) (x2 + y2 + 1)

Q 126

Factorise 8a3 - 2a using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have,  8a3 - 2a
= 2a(4a2 - 1)
2a(2a + 1) (2a - 1)    [{tex}\because{/tex}a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b)]

Q 127

Factorise x2 - {tex}\frac{y^2}{100}{/tex} using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, {tex}x^{2}-\frac{y^{2}}{100}{/tex}
 {tex}x^{2}-\frac{y^{2}}{100}{/tex} = {tex}x^{2}-\left(\frac{y}{10}\right)^{2}{/tex}

= {tex}\left(x+\frac{y}{10}\right)\left(x-\frac{y}{10}\right){/tex} [using identity  a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 128

Factorise 9x2 - (3y + z)2 using the identity a2 - b2 = (a + b) (a - b).

Solution

We have, 9x2 - (3y + z)2 
9x2 - (3y + z)2  = (3x)2 - (3y + z)2 
= (3x + 3y + z) (3x - 3y - z)   [using identity a2 - b2 = (a - b)(a + b)]

Q 129

Carry out the division: 51x3y2z {tex}\div{/tex} 17xyz.

Solution

We have, 
51x3y2z {tex}\div{/tex} 17xy = {tex}\frac{51 x^{3} y^{2} z}{17 x y z}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{17 \times 3 \times x \times x \times x \times y \times y \times z}{17 \times x \times y \times z}{/tex}
= 3x2y

Q 130

Carry out the division: 76x3yz3 {tex}\div{/tex} 19x2y2.

Solution

We have, 76x3yz3 {tex}\div{/tex} 19x2y2
76x3yz3 {tex}\div{/tex} 19x2y2 = {tex}\frac{76 x^{3} y z^{3}}{19 x^{2} y^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4\ \times\ 19\ \times\ x\ \times\ x\ \times\ x\ \times\ y\ \times\ z\ \times\ z\ \times\ z}{19\ \times\ x\ \times\ x\ \times\ y\ \times\ y}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4 x z^{3}}{y}{/tex}

Q 131

Carry out the division: 17ab2c3 {tex}\div{/tex} (-abc2).

Solution

17ab2c3 {tex}\div{/tex} (-abc)2 
= {tex}\frac{17 a b^{2} c^{3}}{-a b c^{2}}{/tex}={tex}\frac{17\ \times\ a\ \times\ b \ \times\ b\ \times\ c\ \times\ c\ \times\ c}{-a \ \times \ b \ \times \ c \times \ c}{/tex}
= -17bc

Q 132

Carry out the division: –121p3q3r3 {tex}\div{/tex} (-11xy2z3).

Solution

-121p3q3r3 {tex}\div{/tex} (-11xy2z3) = {tex}\frac{-121 p^{3} q^{3} r^{3}}{-11 x y^{2} z^{3}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{-11\ \times\ 11\ \times\ p\ \times\ p\ \times\ p\ \times\ q \ \times \ q\ \times\ q\ \times \ r\ \times \ r \ \times \ r}{-11 \ \times \ x \ \times \ y \ \times \ y \ \times \ z \ \times \ z \ \times \ z}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{11 p^{3} q^{3} r^{3}}{x y^{2} z^{3}}{/tex}

Q 133

Perform the division: (3pqr - 6p2q2r2) {tex}\div{/tex} 3pq

Solution

we have, (3pqr - 6p2q2r2) {tex}\div{/tex} 3pq
 It can be written as  {tex}\frac{3 p q r-6 p^{2} q^{2} r^{2}}{3 p q}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3 p q r}{3 p q}-\frac{6 p^{2} q^{2} r^{2}}{3 p q}{/tex}
= {tex}r-\frac{2 \ \times \ 3 \ \times \ p \times \ p \times \ q \ \times \ q\ \times \ r \ \times \ r}{3 \ \times \ p \ \times \ q}{/tex}

  = r - 2pqr2

Q 134

Perform the division: (ax3 - bx2 + cx) {tex}\div{/tex} (-dx).

Solution

We have, (ax3 - bx2 + cx) {tex}\div{/tex} (-dx)

(ax3 - bx2 + cx) {tex}\div{/tex} (-dx) = {tex}\frac{a x^{3}-b x^{2}+c x}{-d x}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{a x^{3}}{-d x}+\frac{b x^{2}}{d x}+\frac{c x}{-d x}{/tex} 
 = {tex}\frac{a \times x \times x \times x}{-d \times x}+\frac{b \times x \times x}{d \times x}+\frac{c \times x}{-d \times x}{/tex}
 = {tex}-\frac{a}{d} x^{2}+\frac{b}{d} x-\frac{c}{d}{/tex}

Q 135

Perform the division: (x3y3 + x2y3 - xy4 + xy) {tex}\div{/tex} xy.

Solution

We have, (x3y3 + x2y3 - xy4 + xy) {tex}\div{/tex} xy
(x3y3 + x2y3 - xy4 + xy) {tex}\div{/tex} xy = {tex}\frac{x^{3} y^{3}\ +\ x^{2} y^{3} \ -\ x y^{4} \ + \ x y}{x y}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x^{3} y^{3}}{x y}+\frac{x^{2} y^{3}}{x y}-\frac{x y^{4}}{x y}+\frac{x y}{x y}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x \ \times \ x \ \times \ x \ \times \ y \ \times \ y \ \times \ y}{x \ \times \ y} \ + \ \frac{x \ \times \ x \ \times \ y \ \times \ y \ \times \ y}{x \ \times \ y} - \frac{x \ \times \ y \ \times \ y \ \times \ y \ \times \ y}{x \ \times \ y} + \frac{x \ \times \ y}{x \ \times \ y}{/tex}
 = x2y2 + xy2 - y3 + 1

Q 136

Perform the division: (-qrxy + pryz - rxyz) {tex}\div{/tex} (-xyz).

Solution

We have, (-qrxy + pryz - rxyz) {tex}\div{/tex} (-xyz) 
(-qrxy + pryz - rxyz) {tex}\div{/tex} (-xyz)  = {tex}\frac{-q r x y\ + \ p r y z \ -\ r x y z}{-x y z}{/tex} 
  = {tex}\frac{-q r x y}{-x y z} + \frac{pr y z}{-x y z} - \frac{r x y z}{-x y z}{/tex} 
 = {tex}\frac{q r}{z}-\frac{p r}{x}+r{/tex}

Q 137

Factorise the expressions and divide them as directed: (x2 - 22x + 117) {tex}\div{/tex} (x - 13).

Solution

We have, (x2 - 22x + 117) {tex}\div{/tex} (x - 13)
 (x2 - 22x + 117) {tex}\div{/tex} (x - 13) = {tex}\frac{x^{2}-22 x+117}{x-13}=\frac{x^{2}-13 x-9 x+117}{x-13}{/tex} 
 = {tex}\frac{x(x-13)-9(x-13)}{x-13}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{(x-13)(x-9)}{x-13}{/tex}
= x - 9

Q 138

Factorise the expressions and divide them as directed: (x3 + x2 - 132x) {tex}\div{/tex} x (x - 11).

Solution

We have, (x3 + x2 - 132x ) {tex}\div{/tex} x(x - 11)
 (x3 + x2 - 132x ) {tex}\div{/tex} x(x - 11) = {tex}\frac{x^{3}\ + \ x^{2}\ -\ 132 x}{x(x\ -\ 11)}=\frac{x\left(x^{2}\ +\ x\ -\ 132\right)}{x(x\ -\ 11)}{/tex}
 = {tex}\frac{x^{2}\ +\ 12 x \ -\ 11 x\ -\ 132}{x\ - \ 11}{/tex}

 = {tex}\frac{x(x\ +\ 12)\ - \ 11(x \ + \ 12)}{x\ -\ 11} {/tex}
 = {tex} \ \frac{(x\ +\ 12)(x \ - \ 11)}{x\ - \ 11}{/tex} 

     = x + 12

Q 139

Factorise the expression and divide them as directed: (2x3 - 12x2 + 16x) {tex}\div{/tex} (x - 2) (x - 4).

Solution

We have, (2x3 - 12x2 + 16x) {tex}\div{/tex} (x - 2) (x - 4)
(2x3 - 12x2 + 16x) {tex}\div{/tex} (x - 2) (x - 4) = {tex}\frac{2 x^{3}\ - \ 12 x^{2} \ +\ 16 x}{(x \ -\ 2)(x\ -\ 4)} = \frac{2 x\left(x^{2}\ -\ 6 x\ +\ 8\right)}{(x\ -\ 2)(x\ -\ 4)}{/tex}

= {tex}\frac{2 x\left(x^{2}\ -\ 4 x\ -\ 2 x\ +\ 8\right)}{(x\ -\ 2)(x\ -\ 4)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2 x[x(x\ -\ 4) \ - \ 2(x\ - \ 4)]}{(x\ -\ 2)(x\ -\ 4)}=\frac{2 x(x\ -\ 4)(x\ -\ 2)}{(x\ -\ 2)(x\ -\ 4)}{/tex} = 2x

Q 140

Factorise the expression and divide them as directed: (9x2 - 4) {tex}\div{/tex} (3x + 2).

Solution

(9x2 - 4 ) {tex}\div{/tex} (3x + 2) = {tex}\frac{9 x^{2}\ -\ 4}{3 x\ +\ 2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(3 x)^{2}\ -\ (2)^{2}}{3 x\ +\ 2}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{(3 x+2)(3 x-2)\ }{3 x\ +\ 2}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
= 3x - 2

Q 141

Factorise the expression and divide them as directed: (3x2 - 48) {tex}\div{/tex} (x - 4).

Solution

We have, (3x2 - 48) {tex}\div{/tex}  (x - 4)
(3x2 - 48) {tex}\div{/tex}  (x - 4) = {tex}\frac{3 x^{2}\ -\ 48}{x\ -\ 4} = \frac{3\left(x^{2}\ -\ 16\right)}{x\ -\ 4}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3\left(x^{2}\ -\ 4^{2}\right)}{x\ -\ 4}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3(x\ +\ 4)(x\ -\ 4)}{x\ -\ 4}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
 = 3(x + 4)

Q 142

Factorise the expression and divide it as directed: (x4 - 16) {tex}\div{/tex} x3 + 2x2 + 4x + 8.

Solution

We have, (x4 - 16) {tex}\div{/tex} x3 + 2x2 + 4x + 8
(x4 - 16) {tex}\div{/tex} x3 + 2x2 + 4x + 8 = {tex}\frac{x^{4}\ -\ 16}{x^{3}\ +\ 2 x^{2}\ +\ 4 x\ +\ 8}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\left(x^{2}\right)^{2}\ -\ 4^{2}}{x^{2}(x\ +\ 2)\ +\ 4(x\ +\ 2)}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
= {tex}\frac{\left(x^{2}\ +\ 4\right)\left(x^{2}\ -\ 4\right)}{\left(x^{2}\ +\ 4\right)(x\ + \ 2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2}\ -\ 2^{2}}{x\ +\ 2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(x\ + \ 2)(x\ - \ 2)}{x\ +\ 2}{/tex}  again  [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
= x - 2

Q 143

Factorise the expression and divide them as directed: (3x4 - 1875) {tex}\div{/tex} (3x2 - 75).

Solution

We have, (3x4 - 1875) {tex}\div{/tex} (3x2 - 75) 
(3x4 - 1875) {tex}\div{/tex} (3x2 - 75) = {tex}\frac{3 x^{4}\ -\ 1875}{3 x^{2}\ -\ 75}=\frac{x^{4}\ - \ 625}{x^{2}\ -\ 25}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\left(x^{2}\right)^{2}\ - \ (25)^{2}}{x^{2}\ - \ 25}{/tex}  [{tex}\because{/tex}{tex}{\left(a\right)^{2}-\ (b)^{2}}=(a+b)(a-b){/tex} ]
 = {tex}\frac{\left(x^{2}\ + \ 25\right)\left(x^{2} \ - \ 25\right)}{\left(x^{2}\ - \ 25\right)}{/tex} 
 = x2 + 25 

Q 144

Factorise {tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}+2-3 x-\frac{3}{x}{/tex}.

Solution

We have, {tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}+2-3 x-\frac{3}{x}{/tex}
{tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}+2-3 x-\frac{3}{x}{/tex} = {tex}x^{2}+\frac{1}{x^{2}}+2 \cdot x \cdot \frac{1}{x}-3\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right){/tex}
 = {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{2}-3\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right){/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 + b2 + 2ab = (a + b)2]
 = {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)\left(x+\frac{1}{x}-3\right){/tex} [taking {tex}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right){/tex} as common]

Q 145

Factorise p4 + q4 + p2q2

Solution

We have, p4 + q4 + p2q2
p4 + q4 + p2q2= p4 + q4 + 2p2q2 - 2p2q2 + p2q2 [adding and subtracting 2p2q2]
= p4 + q4 + 2p2q2 - p2q2
= [(P2)2 + (q2)2 + 2p2q2] - p 2q 2 [using the identity, a2 + b2 + 2ab = (a + b)2]
= (p2 + q2)2 - (pq)2
= (p2 + q2 + pq)(p2 + q2 - pq) [using the identity, a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)

The y-coordinate of any point lying on the X-axis will be zero.

Q 7

Plot the points (4, 4), (1, 3), (4, 2) and (7, 3) on a graph paper and connect them with line segments. Name the shape formed by these points.

Solution


From the graph, it is clear that the shape formed is a rhombus.

Q 8

Write the coordinates of all the points in the given graph.

Solution

A(4, 7), B(7, 4), C (4, 1), D(1, 4), E(3, 5), F(5, 5), G(5, 3), H(3, 3), I(4, 5), J(5, 4), K(4, 3), L(3, 4)

Q 9

The following is a conversion graph of temperature in {tex}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}{/tex} and {tex}^\circ \mathrm{F}{/tex}.
Use the graph to answer the following questions.

  1. Convert 140 {tex}^\circ \mathrm{F}{/tex} to {tex}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}{/tex}.
  2. Convert 20 {tex}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}{/tex} to {tex}^\circ \mathrm{F}{/tex}

Solution

From the graph it is clear that

  1. 140{tex}^\circ \mathrm{F}{/tex} = 60{tex}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}{/tex}.
  2. 20{tex}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}{/tex} = 70{tex}^\circ \mathrm{F}{/tex}
Q 10

The following graph shows a comparison of the approximate sale of items manufactured by a company for the first two years of its operation.

  1. In which months there was a maximum difference in the sale of items of two years?
  2. In which year was there more stability in the sale of items?
  3. In which month the sale remains the same in both the years?
  4. In which month were the sales of the first year less than that of the second year?

Solution
  1. The maximum difference was in June.
  2. There was more stability in sales in the first year.
  3. The sales remained the same in August.
  4. June and November.
Q 11

The given graphs show the progress of two different cyclists during a ride. For each graph, describe the rider’s progress over the period of time.

Solution

After observing the graph, it can be concluded that 

  1. As time passes, the speed of cyclist I decrease steadily.
  2. Speed of cyclist II increases for a short time period, and then increases very slowly.
Q 12

A double bar graph is useful for the ________ of two sets of data.

Q 13

Data represented in a circular form is called a ________ chart.

Q 14

The graph of a linear equation is always a ________ line.

Q 15

The cartesian system used two axes which are ________ to each other.

Q 16

Complete the given table and draw a graph for the equation y = 2x 

x 0 1 2 3 4
y = 2x          
Solution

We have given that  y = 2x
If x = 0, y = 2 (0) = 0
If x = 1, y = 2 (1) = 2
If x = 2, y = 2 (2) = 4
If x = 3, y = 2 (3) = 6
If x = 4, y = 2 (4) = 8
these values can be formulated in table as follows:

x 0 1 2 3 4
y = 2x 0 2 4 6 8

The coordinates of the 5 points are (0, 0),(1, 2), (2, 4), (3, 6), (4, 8)
Take a graph sheet and plot the coordinates of these 5 points on it. Join the points to get a graph.
The graph sheet will look like this.

Q 50

Draw a graph for the radius and circumference of circle using a suitable scale. Form the graph,

  1. find the circumference of the circle when radius is 42 units.
  2. at what radius will the circumference of the circle be 220 units?
Solution

If the radius is 7 units, then the circumference of the circle = 2 {tex}\times\pi\times{/tex} 7
= {tex}2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 7=2 \times 22{/tex} = 44 units [{tex}\because{/tex} circumference of a circles = {tex}2\pi r{/tex}]
If radius is 14 units, then circumference = {tex}2 \times \pi \times 14=28 \times \frac{22}{7}{/tex} = 88 units units
If radius is 21 units, then circumference = {tex}2 \times \pi \times 21=42 \times \frac{22}{7}{/tex} = 132 units
If the radius is 28 units, then circumference = {tex}2 \times \pi \times 28=56 \times \frac{22}{7}{/tex} = 176 units
If the radius is 35 units, then circumference = {tex}2 \times \pi \times 35 = 70 \times \frac{22}{7}{/tex} = 220 units
If the radius is 42 units, then circumference = {tex}2 \times \pi \times 42=84 \times \frac{22}{7}{/tex} = 264 units
Now put these values in the table for the graph,

The radius of the circle 7 14 21 28 35 42
Circumference of the circle 44 88 132 176 220 264

Graph from the above data

From the graph, it is clear that:

  1. The circumference of the circle will be 264 units when the radius is 42 units.
  2. The radius of the circle will be 35 units when the circumference of the circle equals 220 units.

Q 52

The graph given below gives the actual and expected sales of cars of a company for 6 months. Study the graph and answer the questions that follow:

  1. In which month were the actual sales the same as the expected sales?
  2. For which month(s) was (were) the difference in actual and expected sales the maximum?
  3. For which month(s) was (were) the difference in actual and expected sales the least?
  4. What was the total sales of cars in the months-January, February and March?
  5. What is the average sales of cars in the last three months?
  6. Find the ratio of sales in the first three months to the last three months.
Solution

After observing the graph carefully, it is clear that

  1. In April, the actual sales were the same as the expected sales.
  2. In March, the difference in actual and expected sales was the maximum.
  3. In April, the difference in actual and expected sales was the least.
  4. The total sales of cars in the months January, February and March was (75+ 100+ 75) i.e. 250.
  5. The average sales of cars in the last three months is 125 i.e. {tex}\frac{125+100+150}{3}{/tex} = 125
  6. The number of sales of cars in the first three months = 250 and the number of sales of cars in the last three months = 375,
    Therefore the required ratio is 250: 375 i.e. 2 : 3.

Q 55

Study the graph given below of a person who started from his home and returned at the end of the day. Answer the questions that follow.

  1. At what time did the person start from his home?
  2. How much distance did he travel in the first four hours of his journey?
  3. What was he doing from 3 PM to 5 PM?
  4. What was the total distance travelled by him throughout the day?
  5. Calculate the distance covered by him in the first 8 hours of his journey.
  6. At what time did he cover 16 km of his journey?
  7. Calculate the average speed of the man from A to B and B to C.
  8. At what time did he return home?
Solution

After observing the graph carefully, it is clear that

  1. At 10 AM, the person starts from his home.
  2. In the first 4 h (i.e. till 2 PM), he travelled 16 km.
  3. The person was taking rest from 3 PM to 5 PM.
  4. The total distance covered by the person throughout the day was 40 km, i.e. 20 km from A to B and then 20 km from C to D.
  5. The distance covered by him in the first 8 h i.e. from 10 AM to 6 PM was 24 km.
  6. He covered 16 km of his journey at 2 PM.
  7. The total distance covered from A to B = 20 km and the time taken to travel from A to B = 5 h
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} The average speed of the man from A to B = {tex}\frac{20}{5}{/tex} = 4 km/h
    and average speed from B to C = {tex}\frac{0}{2}{/tex} = 0 km/h
  8. He returned home at 10 PM.
Q 56

Plot a line graph for the variables p and q, where p is two times q i.e. the equation is p = 2q. Then, find

  1. the value of p when q = 3.
  2. the value of q when p = 8.
Solution

Given, equation is p = 2q
If p = 2 , then q = {tex}\frac{p}{2}=\frac{2}{2}{/tex}= 1
If p = 4, then q = {tex}\frac{p}{2}=\frac{4}{2}{/tex} = 2
If p = 6, then q = {tex}\frac{p}{2}=\frac{6}{2}{/tex} = 3
If p = 8, then q = {tex}\frac{p}{2}=\frac{8}{2}{/tex} = 4
Hence, table for the graph is given as:

X 2 4 6 8
Y 1 2 3 4

  1. When q = 3, the value of p is 6.
  2. When p = 8, the value of q is 4.

  4. He did not move from 6:30 AM to 9:30 AM and 10 AM to 1 PM. So, he did not move for 6 h.
  5. He have the fastest speed at 1 PM.
Q 71

The following graph shows the journey made by two cyclists, one from town A to B and the other from town B to A.

  1. At what time did cyclist II rest? How long did the cyclist rest?
  2. Was cyclist II cycling faster or slower after the rest?
  3. At what time did the two cyclists meet?
  4. How far had cyclist II travelled when he met cyclist I?
  5. When cyclist II reached town A, how far was cyclist I from town S?
Solution
  1. On the basis of the given graph, cyclist II rest at 8 : 45 AM for 15 min.
  2. Cyclist II is cycling faster after rest as he has covered a distance of 20 km in 1h.
  3. Both cyclists meet at 9:00 AM.
  4. Cyclist II had travelled 20 km, when he met cyclist I.
  5. When cyclist II reached town A, the cyclist I was 10 km from town B.
Q 72

Ajita starts off from home at 07.00 h with her father on a scooter that goes at a uniform speed of 30 km/h and drops her at her school after half an hour. She stays in the school till 13.30 h and takes an auto-rickshaw to return home. The rickshaw has a uniform speed of 10 km/h. Draw the graph for the above situation and also determine the distance of Ajita's school from her house.

Solution

Uniform speed of Ajita's father on scooter = 30 km/h
Time take by her father to drop Ajita to her school = {tex}\frac{1}{2} h{/tex} on 30 min
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Distance between Ajita's school and her home = {tex}30 \times \frac{1}{2}{/tex} = 15 km [{tex}\because{/tex} distance = speed {tex}\times{/tex} time]
Distance cover in 1 min = {tex}\frac{15}{30}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{km}{/tex} = 500m

Q 11

A three-digit number 42x is divisible by 9. Find the value of x.

Solution

It is given that 42x is divisible by 9, the sum of its digits, i.e. 4 + 2 + x must be divisible by 9.
i.e. 6 + x is divisible by 9
i.e. 6 + x = 9
Since x is a digit, therefore 6 + x = 9 or, x = 3.

Q 12

Find the value of A and B if

Solution

From one column A + 4 gives a number whose ones digit is 2. So, A = 8. The value of B can be obtained by solving 2 + B is a number whose one's digit is 1. Therefore, B = 9.

Q 13

Suppose that the division x {tex}\div{/tex} 5 leaves a remainder of 4, and the division x {tex}\div{/tex} 2 leaves a remainder of 1. What must be the one's digit of x?

Solution

The division x {tex}\div{/tex} 5 leaves a remainder of 4
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The ones digit when divided by 5, must leave a remainder of 4
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The one's digit must be either 4 or 9. . . .(1)
Again, the division x {tex}\div{/tex} 2 leaves a remainder of 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x is odd
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Its units digit is odd
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The one's digit must be 1, 3, 5, 7 or 9 ....(2)
In view of (1) and (2)
The one's digit must be 9.

Q 14

If 756x is divisible by 11, where x is a digit find the value of x.

Solution

It is given that 756x is divisible by 11
Sum of digits at odd places = x + 5
Sum of digits at even places = 6 + 7 = 13
Difference = (x + 5) - 13
= x - 8
Now (x - 8) should be equal to 0 or a multiple of 11 (i.e. 11, 22, 33, ..., etc.)
x - 8 = 0
x = 8 or x - 8 = 11
x = 11 + 8
= 19
Since x is a digit so it can take values from 0 - 9
Hence x = 8
The required number is 7568

Q 28

A four-digit number abcd is divisible by 11, if d + b = ________ or ________.

Solution

We know that, a number is divisible by 11, if the difference between the sum of digits at odd places and the sum of its digits at even places is either 0 or a multiple of 11.
Hence, abcd is divisible by 11, if (d + b) - (a + c) = 0, 11, 22, 33,…
Therefore, d + b = a + c
or d + b = 11(a + c)

Find the least value that must be given to number a, so that the number 91876a2 is divisible by 8.

Solution

Given, 91876a2 is divisible by 8.
Since, we know that, if a number is divisible by 8, then the number formed by last 3 digits is divisible by 8.
So, 6a2 is divisible by 8.
Here, a can take values from 0 to 9.
For a = 0, 602 is not divisible by 8.
For a = 1, 612, which is not divisible by 8.
For a = 3, 632 is divisible by 8.
Hence the minimum value of a is 3 to make 91876a2 divisible by 8.

Q 47

If 1AB + CCA = 697 and there is no carry-over in addition, find the value of A + B + C.

Solution

We have, {tex}\begin{array}{r}{1 A B} \\ {+C C A} \\ \hline 6 \ 9 \ 7 \\ \hline\end{array}{/tex}
Since there is no carry-over in addition
1 + C = 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C = 5
A + C = 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A + 5 = 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 4
and B + A = 7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B + 4 = 7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B + 3
Hence, A + B + C = 4 + 3 + 5 = 12

Q 48

A five-digit number AABAA is divisible by 33. Write all the numbers of this form.

Solution

We have given that, a number of the form AABAA is divisible by 33. Then, it is also divisible by 3 and 11.
Since, AABAA is divisible by 3, the sum of its digits is also multiples of 3.
i.e. A + A + B + A + A = 0, 3, 6, 9,...
or 4A + B = 0, 3, 6, 9,12,... (i)
Further, the given number is also divisible by 11,
therefore (2A + B) - 2A = 0, 11, 22,...
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 0, 11, 22, ...
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 0 [{tex}\because{/tex} B is a digit of the given number]
From Eq. (i), we have
4A = 12 or 24 or 36
A = 3, 6, 9
Hence, the required numbers are 33033, 66066 and 99099.

Q 60

Find the value of the letter.

Solution

We have, 

In the ones column,
C - 5 = 8 
Obviously, 13 - 5 = 8
{tex}\therefore{/tex} C = 3
In the ten's column,
B - (C + 1) = 8 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 8 + C + 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 8 + 3 + 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 12
i.e. B = 2
In the hundred's column,

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 4 + B + 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 4 + 2 + 1

In the thousand's column,
8 - A = D
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8 - 7 = D
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} D = 1
Therefore, A = 7, B = 2, C = 3 and D = 1

Q 62

If 212{tex}x{/tex}5 is a multiple of 3 and 11 then find the value of {tex}x{/tex}.

Since it is given that, 212{tex}x{/tex}5 is a multiple of 3,
2 + 1 + 2 + {tex}x{/tex} + 5 = 0, 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10 + {tex}x{/tex} = 0, 3, 6, ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x{/tex} = 2, 5, 8 
Again, 212{tex}x{/tex}5 is a multiple of 11, (2 + 2 + 5) - (1 + {tex}x{/tex}) = 0, 11, 22, 33
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8 - {tex}x{/tex} = 0, 11, 22, ...(ii)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x{/tex} = 8
From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we have
{tex}x{/tex} = 8

i.e. 3 + 1 + k + 2 = 0, 3, 6, 9,12, ...
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k + 6 = 0,3,6,9,12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = 0 or 3, 6, 9

Q 64

Solution

We have given that, 1y3y6 is divisible by 11
Then, we have (1 + 3 + 6) – (y + y) = 0, 11, 22, . . .
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10 - 2y = 0, 11, 22,…
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10 - 2y = 0 [other values give a negative number]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2y = 10
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 5

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8 - x = 0

Q 69

If from a two-digit number, we subtract the number formed by reversing its digits then the result so obtained is a perfect cube. How many such numbers are possible? Write all of them.

Solution

According to the information given in the question, Let ab be any two-digit number. Then, the digit formed by reversing its digits is ba.
Now, ab - ba = (10a + b) - (10b + a)
= (10a - a) + (b - 10b)
= 9a - 9b
= 9(a - b)
Further, since ab - ba is a perfect cube and is a multiple of 9.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The possible value of a - b is 3.
i.e. a = b + 3
Here, a can take value from 0 to 6.
Hence, possible numbers are as follows.
For b = 0, a = 3, i.e. 30
For b = 1, a = 4,  i.e. 41
For b = 2, a = 5, i.e. 52
For b = 3, a = 6, i.e. 63
For b = 4, a = 7, i.e. 74
For b = 5, a = 8, i.e. 85
For b = 6, a = 9, i.e. 96

Q 73

If 148101B095 is divisible by 33, find the value of B.

Solution

Given that the number 148101B095 is divisible by 33
therefore it should be divisible by 3 and 11 both.
Now, the number is divisible by 3, its sum of digits is a multiple of 3.
i.e. 1 + 4 + 8 + 1 + 0 + 1 + B + 0 + 9 + 5 is a multiple of 3.
29 + B = 0, 3, 6, 9,...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 1, 4, 7 
Also, given number is divisible by 11,
therefore
(1 + 8 + 0 + B + 9) - (4 + 1 + 1 + 0 + 5) = 0, 11, 22, ...
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (18 + B) -11 = 0, 11, 22
B + 7 = 0, 11, 22
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B + 7 = 11
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 4 ...(ii)
From Eqs. (i) and (ii),
we get,
 B = 4
Therefore, the required value of B = 4.

Q 74

i.e. (6 + A)-10=0,11,22,...
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A - 4 = 0,11,22, ...
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A - 4 = 0 [{tex}\because{/tex} A is a digit of the given number]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 4

Q 75

If 56x32y is divisible by 18, find the least value of y.

Solution

It is given that, the number 56x32y is divisible by 18, Then, it is also divisible by each factor of 18. 
Thus, it is divisible by 2 as well as 9.
Now, the number is divisible by 2, its unit’s digit must be an even number that Is 0, 2, 4, 6, .....
Therefore, the least value of y is 0.
Again, the number is divisible by 9 also, sum of its digits is a multiple of 9,
i.e. 5 + 6 + x + 3 + 2 + y is a multiple of 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16 + x + y = 9, 18, 27,...
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16 + x = 18
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2, which is the least value of x
therefore, the least value of y = 0
